DEVLOG.md:104:**2026-05-14 14:00:00Z** - **FIX**: post-edc11aa review followups. Two findings: **#1 Medium (delegated_pull logs failures as ok).** `service/core.rs:291` used `ok = !cancelled`, but `handle_delegated_pull` returned `()` and converted `run_delegated_pull` failures into an error-progress message on `tx`. So a real handler failure with the client still connected logged `delegated_pull ok ... errors=N` with `N` unchanged — the operator-facing metric contradicted the actual RPC result. Fix: `handle_delegated_pull` now returns `bool` (true=success, false=failure). The phased error is still surfaced on `tx` as before; the bool is purely the success bit. Caller in `service/core.rs` consumes via `tokio::select!` into `Option<bool>` — `None` = client cancelled, `Some(true)` = handler ok, `Some(false)` = handler failed. `ok = matches!(outcome, Some(true))`; `inc_error` fires only on `Some(false)` (handler failure), not on client cancellation. New regression test `handle_delegated_pull_returns_false_on_handler_failure` drives a blank-host source locator to trip `DelegationRejected` and asserts `false` is returned — pins the return-bool contract without mocking the full pipeline. **#2 Low (release-plan inconsistency).** Top-tracker said §3.1/§3.2/§3.3 closed, but §6 commit-sequence table still labeled steps 9–11 "Not started," step 1 said D4/D5/D6 still open, step 13 still claimed tagging is blocked on "product-owner ack of P1 deferrals," and §7 heading still said D4/D5/D6 are open. All four sites rewritten to reflect 2026-05-13 reality: steps 9–11 carry closing commits (`0d76c4f`, `6e750b9`, `6e750b9`); step 1 says "all taken — D5 modified by owner"; step 13 shows tagging blocked on step 2 only; §7 heading says "all D1–D9 decided, nothing owed anymore" with the section name retained for historical reference. **Tests**: `cargo fmt --all -- --check` clean; `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings` clean; `cargo test --workspace` all pass (+1 delegated_pull regression test).
DEVLOG.md:108:**2026-05-13 18:30:00Z** - **FEAT + SCOPE**: §3.1 / §3.3 / §5.2 / §5.4 closed (D5 modified, D6 default taken, BlitAuth + AI telemetry removed). Three owner decisions landed in one commit set: **(1) §3.1 D5 modified.** Rather than ship `--metrics` as dormant scaffolding, the daemon now emits a one-line stderr summary at end of each push/pull/pull_sync RPC when `--metrics` is on. Format: `[metrics] push ok in 1.23s (push_ops=N pull_ops=N purge_ops=N active=N errors=N)` — rsync/rclone-style operator visibility under systemd / foreground without needing the TUI. `crates/blit-daemon/src/metrics.rs` gains `log_completion(op_kind, duration, ok)` (no-op when disabled — atomics never loaded) and `format_completion_line` (extracted so the format contract is unit-testable without stderr capture). 3 new unit tests in `metrics::tests` pin the line format and error tagging. Counters themselves stay in place and feed future TUI work; the change just gives `--metrics` a real consumer for 0.1.0. **(2) §3.3 D6 default taken.** Phase 4.8 daemon FS-capability work (4.8.2 startup/idle daemon probes + 4.8.3 `blit diagnostics profile` capability probes) explicitly deferred to 0.2.0. `WORKFLOW_PHASE_4.md` §4.8 now carries a scope-note callout marking 4.8.2 and 4.8.3 as deferred; 4.8.1 client-side `fs_capability` cache ships in 0.1.0 as today. **(3) §5.2 BlitAuth removed.** `service BlitAuth { Authenticate(AuthRequest) returns (AuthResponse) }` plus the two messages stripped from `proto/blit.proto`. `RemoteSourceLocator.delegated_credential` (forward-compat auth passthrough hook for delegated pull) also removed — field 10 reserved against future reuse. `crates/blit-cli/src/transfers/remote_remote_direct.rs` no longer sets the field. `DAEMON_CONFIG.md` Trust Model section rewritten to say "no auth, intentional, out of scope — terminate auth at the network edge" (was "no auth in 0.1.0; passthrough field exists for forward-compat"). `docs/plan/REMOTE_REMOTE_DELEGATION_PLAN.md` got a top-of-file auth note flagging body references to BlitAuth/`delegated_credential` as obsolete (body retained as historical design record). **(4) §5.4 AI telemetry removed.** `docs/plan/AI_TELEMETRY_ANALYSIS.md` deleted entirely. References in `docs/plan/README.md`, `docs/plan/PROJECT_STATE_ASSESSMENT.md`, and `CHANGELOG.md` dropped. `WORKFLOW_PHASE_4.md` §4.9 marked as removed from scope. Predictor (§2.8) is unaffected — performance history is still collected; no "analyze my history" feature is planned. **Release-plan tracker:** §3.1 / §3.2 / §3.3 all ✅ closed. §5.2 / §5.4 changed from "deferred" to "removed." `--metrics` decision (D5) modified from default; all other D-decisions taken at their plan defaults. **Net release-blocker count:** §2.6 (hardware-bound 10 GbE benchmark capture) is the last open P0. **Tests**: `cargo test --workspace` all pass (+3 metrics tests); `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings` clean; `cargo fmt --check` clean.
DEVLOG.md:110:**2026-05-13 17:30:00Z** - **FEAT**: §3.2 mDNS TXT enrichment closed (D4 default taken). `crates/blit-core/src/mdns.rs::advertise` now publishes two new TXT keys: `module_count` (always set; the authoritative module count, distinct from `modules` which gets truncated past the ~180-byte TXT cap when a daemon exports many modules) and `delegation_enabled` (`1`/`0`; whether the daemon accepts `DelegatedPull` requests). `AdvertiseOptions` gained a `delegation_enabled: bool` field; the daemon at `crates/blit-daemon/src/main.rs:65` passes `delegation.allow_delegated_pull` through so the advertised value tracks the runtime gate. `MdnsDiscoveredService` gained `.module_count() -> Option<u32>` and `.delegation_enabled() -> Option<bool>` accessors — both return `None` for pre-§3.2 daemons so back-compat is graceful. `blit scan` surfaces both: JSON output adds `module_count` and `delegation_enabled` fields (omitted when absent via `skip_serializing_if`); text output shows `Modules (N): ...` when the names list is truncated relative to the count, `Modules: N (names not advertised)` when only the count came through, and `Delegation: accepts DelegatedPull` when the daemon opts in. 5 new unit tests in `mdns::accessor_tests` pin the wire-shape contract (truthy parsing, absent-field None, modules-accessor non-regression). Verified: `cargo test --workspace` all pass, `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings` clean, `cargo fmt --check` clean. **Tests**: `cargo test --workspace` +5 (was N, now N+5).
DEVLOG.md:116:**2026-05-07 14:00:00Z** - **DOCS**: Followup #2 catching review of `8d43e4d` — the RELEASE_PLAN_v2 itself was still presenting closed P0s as open work, which was a serious risk because `docs/plan/README.md` points new agents at it as the live source of truth for what remains before shipping. **Medium**: added a top-of-file "Closure tracker (last updated 2026-05-07)" block with a one-row-per-section table showing closing commits for §2.1/§2.2/§2.3/§2.4/§2.5/§2.7/§2.8 and §4, plus pending markers for §2.6 (hardware-bound benchmark — last open P0) and §3.1/§3.2/§3.3 (P1, doc-only). Each P0 section now has its own per-section `**Status (2026-05-07):** ✅ Closed <commit>` block at the top so a reader landing on a specific section sees current state without scrolling back to the top. §2.5 line 265 also still showed the old `blit completions remote <prefix>` syntax in the recommendation paragraph — corrected to the actual `<REMOTE> [--prefix <STR>] [--files] [--dirs]` form, matching the existing line-21 manpage. §6 commit-sequence rewritten as a status table with closing commits per row (P0 done except step 2 §2.6; P1 deferrable). §7 decisions table flipped from "Default recommendation" / "Owner" columns to "Default" / "Outcome" with closing commits where taken — D1/D2/D3/D7/D8/D9 ✅ taken, D4/D5/D6 still open. Test totals updated from the 2026-05-04 baseline (383) to current (407, after R41-R45 review-fix coverage). Footer "Last updated" + "Next review" rewritten. **Low**: `docs/plan/REMOTE_TRANSFER_PARITY.md:83` Success Criteria still said `blit-cli mirror -p -v local remote`; updated to `blit mirror`. Unlike POST_REVIEW_FIXES.md, this file is listed as still-applicable design in `docs/plan/README.md`, so a stale binary name there would mislead. **Tests**: `cargo test --workspace` 407 pass / 0 fail (no code touched).
DEVLOG.md:126:**2026-05-05 18:30:00Z** - **ACTION**: §2.8 phase 2 of `docs/plan/RELEASE_PLAN_v2_2026-05-04.md` — orchestrator and `blit profile` now actually query the predictor. Three load-bearing changes: (1) `crates/blit-core/src/orchestrator/orchestrator.rs` introduces a `plan_done = Instant::now()` checkpoint between the planner stage and the pipeline so `planner_duration_ms` and `transfer_duration_ms` are correctly attributed; pre-fix both fields fed `record_performance_history` got the same whole-run time, so v1 predictor training collapsed into a single regressor. (2) Just before kicking off `execute_sink_pipeline`, the orchestrator queries `predictor.predict(DurationKind::Total/Planner/Transfer, ...)` with the just-scanned `all_headers.len()` and `total_bytes`; populates `LocalMirrorSummary.predictor_estimate` (new field of type `PredictorEstimate { planner_ms, transfer_ms, total_ms, observations, fallback_depth }` in `summary.rs`). `--verbose` now emits two lines: a "Predictor estimate: ..." pre-run line and a "Predictor delta: ..." post-run line showing predicted-vs-actual percent error per phase. (3) `crates/blit-cli/src/profile.rs::run_profile` now calls `predictor.coefficients_for(mode-only, ...)` for both Copy and Mirror, surfaces the result as a coefficients block in human output (`alpha_ms_per_file`, `beta_ms_per_mb`, `gamma_ms` per planner/transfer pair) and as a `predictor.{copy,mirror}` JSON object under `--json`. Smoke test: `blit profile --json` returns the new `predictor` key (null when no v2 profile yet); `blit copy` --verbose under streaming-planner workloads will emit the new predictor lines once 5+ observations accumulate. Tiny fast-path extension (the planner-overhead-dominance signal in the plan) deferred to post-0.1.0 — extending the fast_path scan budget risks edge cases on memory-bounded large-tree workloads, and the predictor's now-load-bearing model is only freshly trained on v2 data; revisit once we have multi-week observability under the new schema. **Tests**: `cargo test --workspace` 404 pass / 0 fail.
DEVLOG.md:176:**2026-05-02 13:15:00Z** - **ACTION**: Closed Round 6 review findings R6-F1 through R6-F3 from `docs/reviews/followup_review_2026-05-02.md`. **R6-F1** (Medium — DoS via per-entry size): `apply_pull_tar_shard` was preallocating with `Vec::with_capacity(header.size as usize)`, where `header.size` came from the daemon-authored `TarShardHeader.files`. A daemon could keep the shard `archive_size` under the 256 MiB cap while putting `u64::MAX` in a per-file header, forcing a huge initial allocation. Now validates `entry.size() == header.size` and `header.size <= shard.declared_size <= MAX_TAR_SHARD_BYTES` before any allocation, then uses `Vec::try_reserve_exact` so a still-pathological size returns `AllocError` instead of aborting. **R6-F2** (Medium — silent partial success): if the gRPC stream ended with `active_shard = Some(_)` (Header sent, no Complete), the receive loop dropped the buffered shard and returned success. Extracted the post-loop check into `ensure_no_open_shard` and made it bail with a wire protocol error — same treatment as `FileData` without a preceding `FileHeader`. **R6-F3** (Medium — metadata desync): manual tar-shard writes weren't applying `header.mtime_seconds` or `header.permissions`, so a forced-gRPC pull of many small files succeeded byte-for-byte but left destination mtimes at "now", causing the next size+mtime sync to re-transfer everything. Now applies mtime via `filetime::set_file_mtime` when `> 0` and Unix permissions when nonzero, mirroring `FsTransferSink`. 3 new unit tests in `tar_shard_safety_tests` (rejects oversized per-entry size, rejects open shard at end-of-stream, preserves mtime). **Tests**: `cargo fmt`; `cargo test --workspace` (158 lib tests + integration suites, all green).
DEVLOG.md:186:**2026-05-02 04:15:00Z** - **STEP 4A (PullSync wire migration to TransferOperationSpec)**: With no backwards-compat constraint into the next release, replaced the bool-soup `PullSyncHeader` (5 booleans for comparison mode + separate fields for resume/mirror/force_grpc) with the unified `TransferOperationSpec` defined in step 2. `ClientPullMessage::Header` is now `ClientPullMessage::Spec`. `PullSyncHeader` deleted from the proto entirely. Daemon parses the spec and translates its `ComparisonMode` enum onto the existing internal `CompareMode` via a new `compare_mode_to_internal` helper that also splits out `IgnoreExisting` as the orthogonal axis it actually is. Client (`RemotePullClient::pull_sync`) constructs the spec from CLI args, advertising full `PeerCapabilities` (resume, tar shards, data plane TCP, filter spec — all true), and defaulting `mirror_mode` to `MirrorMode::FilteredSubset` rather than the old "any-mirror-mode-true" boolean. Filter not yet honored on the daemon — that's step 4B. **The bool-soup → typed-enum win is real**: nonsensical combinations like `size_only=true && checksum=true && ignore_times=true` are no longer constructible. **Tests**: 214 pass (unchanged) — pure wire-shape migration, behavior preserved end-to-end.
DEVLOG.md:196:**2026-05-02 01:15:00Z** - **F1 (path safety)**: Centralized receive-side path validation in `blit-core/src/path_safety.rs`. Two helpers: `validate_wire_path(s) -> PathBuf` (pure validation, normalized form) and `safe_join(root, s) -> PathBuf` (validate + join, with empty input returning root unchanged for the single-file destination case). Rejects: `..` components (not just substrings — the previous tar-shard `rel.contains("..")` check was too weak and also rejected legitimate filenames containing literal `..`), absolute paths, Windows drive prefixes (`C:\…`), UNC and DOS device paths (`\\?\…`, `\\server\share\…`), root components, embedded NUL bytes. Migrated `pull.rs::sanitize_relative_path` and daemon `service/util.rs::resolve_relative_path` / `resolve_manifest_relative_path` to thin wrappers that delegate to the shared module — single chokepoint, no drift between sanitizers. Applied at all five receive-side sites the review identified: `sink.rs:218` (streamed file write), `sink.rs:404` + `:426` (tar shard entry validation + per-entry join), `sink.rs:462` (resume block write), `sink.rs:498` (block completion), and the gRPC fallback block-hash path in `pull.rs:532`. Wire-boundary defense in depth: `pipeline.rs::read_file_header` and per-entry path read in `read_tar_shard` validate at the moment paths arrive off the socket, so unsafe headers never enter the FileHeader stream. **Tests**: 209 passed (was 185 baseline) — 16 new `path_safety::tests` covering the validator surface (`..`, absolute Unix, Windows drive, UNC, NUL, valid `..`-containing filenames, deep paths, Unicode, trailing slash, just-dot), 8 new sink-level integration tests confirming `FsTransferSink::write_file_stream` rejects malicious wire paths before any filesystem write and that the legitimate single-file empty-path case still works. **Verification**: `cargo check --workspace` clean; `cargo test --workspace` 0 failures.
DEVLOG.md:200:**2026-05-01 23:43:57Z** - **REVIEW**: Completed a repo-level codebase review and saved it to `docs/reviews/codebase_review_2026-05-01.md`. Highest-priority findings: receive-side path sanitization is incomplete across streamed files, tar shards, and resume block records; daemon `use_chroot` is documented/configured but not enforced; filtered mirror delete semantics need an explicit product decision; metrics lifecycle and endpoint behavior need hardening. Added a short `TODO.md` follow-up section and marked Phase 4 as in progress with the review artifact linked. **Verification**: `cargo check --workspace` passed; `cargo test --workspace` passed with 185 tests after rerunning outside the sandbox to allow local test port binding.
DEVLOG.md:202:**2026-04-24 03:15:00Z** - **FIX**: Fixed single-file remote push crash — `blit copy FILE server:/mod/` previously died with "opening FILE/ during payload planning: Not a directory". Root cause was two-sided: on the client, `filter_readable_headers` and `build_tar_shard` computed `source_root.join(&rel)` where `rel=""` for single-file sources, which on Unix preserves a trailing `/` and makes `File::open` reject with ENOTDIR. On the daemon, `resolve_relative_path` folded empty paths to `"."` (which broke `module.path.join("")` = wanted single file, but got `module.path/.`), and the subsequent `module.path.join(empty_rel)` added another trailing `/`. Fix applies the `rel.is_empty() → use root directly` pattern in three places on the client side (matching `FsTransferSource::open_file`'s existing handling), and adds two new helpers on the daemon side: `resolve_manifest_relative_path` preserves empty as empty for per-file manifest entries, and `resolve_dest_path(base, rel)` handles the empty-rel join case. Swapped all four push-side `resolve_relative_path` callers and three `module.path.join` sites. Regression tests in `crates/blit-cli/tests/remote_push_single_file.rs` pin both the container-dir (`blit copy FILE server:/mod/`) and rename (`blit copy FILE server:/mod/new.txt`) cases. Annotated all four `docs/bugs/*.md` files with their resolution status. **Tests**: 173 passed (+2 remote push single-file), clippy clean.
DEVLOG.md:242:**2025-01-28 11:00:00Z** - **ACTION**: Reverted temp file approach for atomic copies after design review. Temp files create orphan cleanup complexity - if process crashes, orphans need manual or automatic cleanup. Instead, implementing resumable transfers: partial files are left with real filename, and next run with `--resume` can verify/resume from where it stopped using block-level checksums. This aligns with RELIABLE principle (correctness > speed) while keeping SIMPLE (no user intervention for cleanup).
DEVLOG.md:250:**2025-01-26 13:00:00Z** - **ACTION**: Removed all backwards-compatibility code from `DataPlaneSession` per "no tech debt" directive (project hasn't shipped yet). Eliminated `SessionBuffer` enum, legacy `from_stream()` signature, `connect_with_pool` wrapper, and single-buffer fallback path. Now `DataPlaneSession::from_stream` and `::connect` both require `Arc<BufferPool>` and always use double-buffered I/O. Updated callers: `MultiStreamSender::connect` passes pool directly (removed `Some()` wrapper), `pull.rs::handle_pull_stream` creates a pool sized for single-stream operation. Clean compile, all buffer pool tests pass. **Tests**: `cargo check --workspace`; `cargo test -p blit-core buffer`.
DEVLOG.md:260:**2025-01-21 13:00:00Z** - **ACTION**: Verified and documented remote↔remote transfer support. Implementation was already complete via `RemoteTransferSource` abstraction in `blit-core/src/remote/transfer/source.rs`. Removed debug print statement from `RemoteTransferSource::open_file`. Existing integration test `remote_remote.rs` covers dual-daemon server-to-server copy. Updated all integration tests (`remote_parity.rs`, `remote_pull_mirror.rs`, `remote_tcp_fallback.rs`, `remote_move.rs`, `remote_remote.rs`) to include `--yes` flag for mirror/move commands following the destructive operation prompts implementation. **Tests**: All remote integration tests now use `--yes` to skip confirmation prompts.
DEVLOG.md:286:**2025-11-07 22:10:00Z** - **PLAN**: Captured the remote transfer parity workflow (`docs/plan/REMOTE_TRANSFER_PARITY.md`) and promoted it to a P0 in TODO. The plan folds in missing v1 capabilities: shared `remote::transfer` modules, auto-tuned chunk/stream sizing, larger data-plane buffers with zero-copy, and hybrid transport for pull. Next up is implementing the shared modules + scheduler wiring, extending `PullChunk` with negotiation/summary, then rewriting the daemon/client pull paths on top of the shared transport before adding parity integration tests.

docs/DECISIONS.md:67:## D-2026-06-11-2 — Design-review queue ratified in full; Pull-RPC delete; zero_copy gets a FAST evaluation
docs/DECISIONS.md:68:- Decision: All Phase C slices (`AUDIT_REPORT_2026-06-11_DESIGN.md`) ratified as proposed and entered into REVIEW.md in the proposed order. Embedded decisions: (a) **W2.4** — the deprecated Pull RPC is deleted once W2.3 has harvested its multi-stream pattern; criterion applied: not needed for FAST/SIMPLE/RELIABLE in any scenario. (b) **W8.1** — `zero_copy.rs` is **excluded** from the dead-code deletion sweep; owner judges it has FAST potential; disposition is an evaluation slice (`w8-1b`) that either produces a plan doc to wire splice into the receive pipeline or concludes deletion. (c) **W2.3** — writing the multi-stream-pull plan doc is authorized (no code before Status: Active).
docs/DECISIONS.md:72:## D-2026-06-12-1 — zero_copy.rs: delete (w8-1b verdict)
docs/DECISIONS.md:73:- Decision: `zero_copy.rs` is deleted rather than wired in. The w8-1b evaluation (`docs/plan/ZERO_COPY_RECEIVE_EVAL.md`) recommended deletion and the owner agreed (2026-06-12 session). The deletion executes inside w8-1 once the w5-1 sentinel (lib.rs) is graded — it is no longer excluded from that sweep.
docs/DECISIONS.md:75:- Supersedes: D-2026-06-11-2 item (b) (zero_copy exclusion from W8.1 was pending this evaluation; the evaluation is done).
docs/DECISIONS.md:79:- Why: owner (30-year IT veteran, not a developer) judges the fragmentation — one engine for local, hand-wired loops for push/pull, three competing static stream-count tables, no live tuning — is the root of the "local↔local 10× slower than local→daemon" class of drift; a single engine makes that class impossible by construction and gives the LLM agent one place to update. Ground-up rebuild was judged too much; convergence on the existing shared leaf is the FAST/SIMPLE/RELIABLE fit. The adaptive substrate was purpose-built by an earlier Fable session as C's foundation, so building A on it does not paint the design into a corner.
docs/DECISIONS.md:81:- Supersedes: the "ground-up redesign" framing of the 2026-06-14 open question recorded in STATE.md (that open question is now closed); `MULTISTREAM_PULL.md` as a standalone plan (goal absorbed into `UNIFIED_TRANSFER_ENGINE.md` slice `ue-1d`).
docs/DECISIONS.md:96:- Why: review found the Active plan's direction is sound but several slices need tightening before code starts: streaming initial planning was hidden inside `ue-1c`, local fast paths need to become engine-owned strategies, work-stealing is observable behavior, wire compatibility needs concrete shape, and pull parity gates must wait for multistream pull.
docs/DECISIONS.md:102:- Supersedes: `docs/plan/UNIFIED_TRANSFER_ENGINE.md` (v1, Active → Superseded) and the review candidates `REV2.md` / `REV3.md` (Draft → Superseded) — all by `REV4.md`. Lifts D-2026-06-20-4's implementation freeze (the plan decision is now made). Does **not** supersede the convergence direction (D-2026-06-20-1), the four bound parameters (D-2026-06-20-2), or the H10b veto (D-2026-06-20-3). ~~The D-2026-06-20-1 warmup/size-gate cleanup remains an open owner question, untouched here.~~ *(Resolved 2026-07-04 — cleanup applied in place; see the edited D-2026-06-20-1.)*
docs/DECISIONS.md:115:- Decision: The two Windows-session commits that don't build in isolation (`9f37a7a` clippy baseline carrying a stray `pull.rs` deletion, `48c5a11` win-1) stay on `master` as pushed; no rebase, no force-push. `git bisect` runs must skip them (both are documented in the ue-r2-1h finding doc and DEVLOG). This closes the erratum question opened 2026-07-04.
docs/DECISIONS.md:117:- Supersedes: nothing (closes the STATE.md "commit erratum" blocked item).
docs/DECISIONS.md:126:- Why: the codex plan review is complete (5/5 accepted + fixed, records `219cecf`) and the plan binds the measured small-file/mixed ceiling gaps (`docs/bench/10gbe-2026-07-05/`) to the owner's ceiling-driven principle. The other four 10 GbE gate declarations (ue-1, ue-2, zero-copy a/b/c, REV4 → Shipped) were NOT part of this go and stay in STATE.md Blocked.
docs/DECISIONS.md:129:## D-2026-07-05-1 — One transfer path; direction-invariance by construction; SMALL_FILE_CEILING paused
docs/DECISIONS.md:130:- Decision: All byte transfer in blit must flow through ONE `TransferSession` implementation — direction, initiator, and CLI verb select *roles* (SOURCE/DESTINATION), never code. The per-direction drivers (client push driver, daemon push-receive, client pull driver, daemon pull-send, delegated-pull driver, separate local orchestration) and the `Push`/`PullSync` RPCs are deleted when the migration completes — owner, 2026-07-05: "ONE BLOCK OF CODE that does the transfer. no POSSIBILITY OF ANYTHING EVER using anything else because anything else does not exist"; "I NEVER see a situation where pull is faster than push or vice versa... because of something blit did." Benchmark methodology corollary: cross-direction performance comparisons are valid only on symmetric endpoints ("tmp on one side, spinning rust on the other is not a valid test"); tmpfs cells are wire-reference only. Plan: `docs/plan/ONE_TRANSFER_PATH.md` (Draft; no code until the owner flips it Active). `docs/plan/SMALL_FILE_CEILING.md` is **paused** effective immediately at sf-2 (done): sf-3a and later slices are blocked until ONE_TRANSFER_PATH ships, then resume/re-derive against the unified baseline (owner delegated this sequencing: "I DO NOT CARE. FIX IT.").
docs/DECISIONS.md:132:- Supersedes: the post-REV4 residue item "pull 1s-start restructuring" (STATE Queue item 4 — absorbed by ONE_TRANSFER_PATH's streaming-manifest choreography); SMALL_FILE_CEILING's queue position (paused, not superseded — its principle D-2026-07-04-4 stands); ~~and, effective only at ONE_TRANSFER_PATH's cutover slice (otp-10), REV4 §Constraints' "mixed old/new peers must negotiate down" rule (annotated in place; until that slice lands the rule governs)~~ **(the "only at cutover" scoping is superseded by D-2026-07-05-2 — no version compatibility, ever, effective immediately)**. The bounded-unilateral dial contract (D-2026-06-20-1/-2) is NOT superseded — it carries into the unified session unchanged.
docs/DECISIONS.md:134:## D-2026-07-05-2 — No version compatibility, ever: same-build peers only
docs/DECISIONS.md:135:- Decision: Blit has NO version-compatibility obligation of any kind, in any direction, at any time — owner standing rule, restated with force 2026-07-05: "backward compatibility is NOT a consideration. I expect blit 1.2.3 not to be able to talk to blit-daemon 1.2.3.1. period. same build only. do not engineer tech debt into an unshipped product." Client and daemon interoperate only when built from the same source; the wire handshake must REFUSE a mismatched peer outright at session open (exact protocol/build identity — mechanism specified in ONE_TRANSFER_PATH otp-1 and pinned by test). Feature-capability bits that exist to tolerate version skew ("advisory until both peers advertise support", `supports_stream_resize`-style flags) are dead weight and go away with the unified session. NOT affected: the receiver capacity profile (runtime capacity of the receiving machine, D-2026-06-20-1/-2) — that is hardware negotiation, not version negotiation.
docs/DECISIONS.md:136:- Why: REV4 §Constraints carried a written "mixed old/new peers must negotiate down" rule while the owner's contrary rule lived only in chat; the ONE_TRANSFER_PATH plan review then resolved the document conflict in favor of the written rule ("governs until cutover"). Wrong direction — recording the owner's rule as a decision ends the unrecorded-intent-loses-to-stale-paper failure mode.
docs/DECISIONS.md:137:- Supersedes: REV4 §Constraints mixed-version clause (annotated in place, effective immediately — not at cutover); SMALL_FILE_CEILING §Constraints "mixed-version peers keep working via existing negotiation" clause and sf-6's mixed-version-test deliverable (annotated); the "effective only at ONE_TRANSFER_PATH's cutover slice" scoping inside D-2026-07-05-1's Supersedes line (the supersession is immediate and total); ONE_TRANSFER_PATH's Non-goals compat wording (rewritten same commit).
docs/DECISIONS.md:139:## D-2026-07-05-3 — Zero-copy receive unparked: revisit gate declared met (UNAS rig)
docs/DECISIONS.md:140:- Decision: The D-2026-06-12-1 revisit gate ("receive-side CPU saturation") is **declared met by the owner** (2026-07-05): a UniFi UNAS 8 Pro daemon target whose CPU cannot saturate 10 GbE even from SSD cache. Zero-copy receive is unparked as sanctioned FAST work. Two clarifications: (a) the dead `zero_copy.rs` module still gets deleted as ratified — its EAGAIN busy-wait draft is a rewrite, not a revival (eval doc); (b) the capability returns the one-path way (owner exchange 2026-07-05): a **runtime-selected write strategy inside the unified receive sink** — the eval doc's revisit design (`AsyncFd`-readiness splice loop beside the buffered relay, selected when the reader is a raw TcpStream and the payload is a file record, buffered relay as universal fallback), capability-gated by kernel/fs support, identical in both roles — never a side path. Sequenced after ONE_TRANSFER_PATH's cutover (otp-10) as its own slice set; the UNAS is the measurement rig and the symmetric-endpoint benchmark rule (D-2026-07-05-2 era methodology) applies to its cells.
docs/DECISIONS.md:141:- Why: the 10 GbE session showed skippy's 32-core receiver at 1.43 cores — gate not met on that rig — but the gate was always about CPU-bound receivers, and the owner now operates one. On a CPU-bound receiver, cutting the userspace copy is exactly the FAST lever the eval preserved design notes for.
docs/DECISIONS.md:142:- Supersedes: the STATE Blocked "zero-copy option a/b/c" question and the "zero-copy revisit verdict" item among the four 10 GbE owner declarations (both resolved by this entry); amends D-2026-06-12-1's revisit-gate framing from "10 GbE benchmarks showing receive-side CPU saturation" to "a CPU-bound receiver exists" (annotated in the eval doc). D-2026-06-12-1's deletion of the dead module stands.

docs/audit/AUDIT_REPORT_2026-06-11_DESIGN.md:47:## W2 — Pull-direction parity (the FAST flagship)

docs/audit/AUDIT_REPORT_2026-06-11_DESIGN.md:111:## W5 — Errors & failure text (RELIABLE)
docs/audit/AUDIT_REPORT_2026-06-11_DESIGN.md:182:  embedded**: `zero_copy.rs` (219 lines, Linux splice) — delete, or keep and
docs/audit/AUDIT_REPORT_2026-06-11_DESIGN.md:183:  file a FAST plan to actually wire it? [deadcode-core-abandoned-foundation-modules]
docs/audit/AUDIT_REPORT_2026-06-11_DESIGN.md:238:## Proposed queue order (rationale: cheap-and-load-bearing first, then the FAST program, with test nets landing before the behavior they protect)
docs/audit/AUDIT_REPORT_2026-06-11_DESIGN.md:261:- [ ] **W8.1**: `zero_copy.rs` — delete, or keep + plan to wire splice (FAST)?
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:37:   pull fallback loops, `jobs watch`, and the TUI forwarder forever. RELIABLE.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:42:   the auto-tuner's `tcp_buffer_size` is silently discarded for pulls. FAST + RELIABLE.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:48:   next contributor. RELIABLE.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:54:   pull implementation lives in the *deprecated* Pull RPC. SIMPLE + FAST, by
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:61:   No pipeline carries a denominator, so percent/ETA is impossible everywhere. RELIABLE.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:66:   shutdown (the prometheus bridge does). RELIABLE.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:80:   explicit decode limits anywhere; residual open question: tar-shard manifest
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:93:blit-core alone: five entire modules (`errors`, `tar_stream`, `zero_copy`,
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:143:No module owns connection construction; ownership is smeared across five crates with one bright spot (mDNS, genuinely owned by crates/blit-core/src/mdns.rs). The gRPC control-plane channel is built three independent times with identical 30s+30s deadlines and an identical pasted audit comment (blit-core pull.rs:239, blit-core push/client/mod.rs:307, blit-app client.rs:37) — agreeing today, structurally guaranteed to diverge on the next fix, since blit-core cannot reuse blit-app's helper. The sharpest problem is direction asymmetry on the TCP data plane: push sockets get NODELAY + keepalive + auto-tuned buffers on both ends, while the entire pull/pull-sync direction (client connect at pull.rs:1710 and all three daemon accept paths) runs on raw kernel defaults, silently discarding the auto-tuner's tcp_buffer_size and leaving Nagle enabled — a direct FAST violation invisible to users. Both data-plane TcpStream::connect calls are also the only unbounded connects left in the codebase, reproducing the exact hang the audit-2 wave fixed for gRPC. Finally, the daemon's HTTP/2 keepalive (main.rs:138) has no client-side counterpart on any channel, and the workspace relies on tonic's unstated 4 MiB decode limit, guarded only by an undocumented 1 MiB fallback-chunk clamp.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:147:- Unbounded data-plane connects: both TCP data-plane client connects — push (crates/blit-core/src/remote/transfer/data_plane.rs:92) and pull (crates/blit-core/src/remote/pull.rs:1710) — call TcpStream::connect with no timeout, while every control-plane connect is bounded two or three times over. A firewalled/black-holed data port hangs the transfer for the OS SYN timeout (the very 60-127s failure mode the audit-2 comments document and fixed only for gRPC). RELIABLE violation: it does not fail plainly, it hangs.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:148:- Push/pull socket asymmetry: TCP_NODELAY, SO_KEEPALIVE, and auto-tuned SO_SNDBUF/SO_RCVBUF are set on push-direction sockets only (client data_plane.rs:98-121, daemon push/data_plane.rs:118-120). The pull and pull-sync data sockets on BOTH ends (pull.rs:1710 client; daemon pull.rs accept_and_wrap_sinks, pull_sync.rs both paths) get kernel defaults — Nagle on, no keepalive, default windows. FAST violation: pull throughput is systematically untuned relative to push on identical hardware, and the auto-tuner's tcp_buffer_size output is silently discarded for pulls.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:149:- No client-side HTTP/2 keepalive on any of the three channel builders, while the daemon server has 30s/20s (main.rs:138-139). A daemon that dies silently mid long-lived stream (TUI Subscribe — SUBSCRIBE_OPEN_TIMEOUT bounds only the open; push/pull control streams) leaves the client waiting indefinitely unless a stall guard happens to cover that path. The one-sided fix from audit-1 was never mirrored.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:178:- `gRPC connect deadline (inner+outer)` = Duration::from_secs(30), hardcoded twice here, twice in push/client/mod.rs:309-310, named CONNECT_TIMEOUT only in blit-app/src/client.rs:24 (crates/blit-core/src/remote/pull.rs:241) — RELIABLE: the value is fine but exists as five literals + one constant; a policy change cannot land atomically.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:179:- `http2_keepalive_interval / http2_keepalive_timeout (server only)` = 30s / 20s (crates/blit-daemon/src/main.rs:138) — RELIABLE: configured on the daemon server only; none of the three client channel builders sets keepalive, so a daemon that vanishes mid-stream (Subscribe, push/pull control stream) is invisible to clients — the asymmetric half of the leak the audit-1 comment fixed server-side.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:180:- `tonic decode limit (implicit)` = ABSENT — rg for max_decoding_message_size/max_encoding_message_size over all crates returns zero hits; everyone inherits tonic's 4 MiB default (proto/blit.proto:1) — RELIABLE: data frames are clamped to 1 MiB (grpc_fallback.rs:102,110-112) so they fit, but nothing protects manifest-shaped messages or future growth; a >4 MiB message fails with tonic's opaque ResourceExhausted, not a plain Blit error.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:182:- `DATA_PLANE_ACCEPT_TIMEOUT / DATA_PLANE_TOKEN_TIMEOUT (and 3 re-definitions)` = 30s / 15s — re-defined at pull.rs:698-699, pull_sync.rs:597-598, pull_sync.rs:719-720 (crates/blit-daemon/src/service/push/data_plane.rs:71) — RELIABLE: values agree today only by copy-discipline; four definitions of one policy.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:183:- `SOURCE_CONNECT_TIMEOUT` = 30s (crates/blit-daemon/src/service/delegated_pull.rs:35) — SIMPLE: third nested 30s timeout around a connect already bounded twice inside RemotePullClient::connect — harmless but evidence nobody trusts (or knows) the inner layers.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:184:- `auto-tune bandwidth ladder` = chunk 32 MiB if >=6 Gbps else 16 MiB (26-32); streams 6/4/2 at >8/>3/else Gbps (45-55); tcp_buffer 8/4/1 MiB + prefetch 32/16/8 (57-67); max_streams 8 (72) (crates/blit-core/src/auto_tune/mod.rs:26) — SIMPLE-aligned (runtime adaptation, zero user knobs) but FAST-compromised: tcp_buffer_size is applied only on the push client socket (data_plane.rs:110-121); daemon accept sides and the entire pull direction never receive it, so the tuner's output is silently dropped for half the configurations.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:185:- `PlannedPayloads.chunk_bytes` = TCP-tuned chunk size (16-32 MiB) embedded in the transport-agnostic payload plan (crates/blit-core/src/remote/transfer/payload.rs:112) — FAST/SIMPLE: layering leak (seed example) — when the plan is consumed by gRPC fallback sinks it is clamped to 1 MiB at payload.rs:251, making the tuned value inert on that transport.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:187:- `GRPC_FALLBACK_CHUNK_BYTES` = 1 MiB (clamp at lines 110-112) (crates/blit-core/src/remote/transfer/grpc_fallback.rs:102) — RELIABLE: this clamp is the only thing keeping data frames under the implicit 4 MiB decode limit; the dependency is undocumented at either end.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:188:- `chunk floor 64 KiB` = 64 * 1024, repeated at push/client/mod.rs:126, daemon pull.rs:685, pull_sync.rs:636, pull_sync.rs:758 (crates/blit-core/src/remote/transfer/data_plane.rs:66) — SIMPLE: five literal copies of one floor.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:192:- `SUBSCRIBE_OPEN_TIMEOUT / SNAPSHOT_FETCH_TIMEOUT / F1_DISCOVERY_SCAN_TIMEOUT (TUI, recorded only)` = 30s / 30s (2426) / 1500ms (2515) (crates/blit-tui/src/main.rs:5639) — RELIABLE: subscribe bounds only stream open; stream liveness then depends entirely on the daemon-side keepalive because the client channel has none.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:201:- /home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-core/src/remote/push/client/mod.rs:110 — MultiStreamSender::connect — opens N parallel DataPlaneSession::connect sockets — Per-stream loop at lines 133-150; stream count from auto-tune (max_streams clamp at 637-640); chunk_bytes/tcp_buffer_size/prefetch all flow from TuningParams at call site 644-655.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:227:- HEADLINE — no client channel in the workspace configures HTTP/2 keepalive, TCP keepalive, or a per-RPC deadline (verified: the only keepalive is the daemon server, main.rs:138-139; no Endpoint anywhere calls .timeout()/.tcp_keepalive()/.http2_keep_alive_interval()). Consequence: every long-lived client-side gRPC await — delegation progress (blit-app/src/transfers/remote.rs:734, :878), push response forwarder (blit-core/src/remote/push/client/helpers.rs:245 feeding mod.rs:439), all three pull fallback receive loops (blit-core/src/remote/pull.rs:330, :505, :790), RemoteFileStream (pull.rs:1232), `blit jobs watch` at default --timeout-secs=0 (blit-cli/src/jobs.rs:305), and the TUI forwarder (blit-tui/src/main.rs:5717) — hangs forever against a silently-dead daemon (kill -9 survives via FIN, but lid-close/partition/NAT-drop does not). Violates RELIABLE ('fails plainly').
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:231:- Policy smear / drift hazard: the accept(30s)/token(15s) pair is declared four times under four names (twice in pull_sync.rs, where a comment falsely claims reuse); the 30s connect bound is declared three times (one named, two inline) plus a redundant third wrap in delegated_pull.rs:280; the open-RPC bound was re-invented per consumer (TUI 30s, bridge 8s, CLI admin verbs and jobs.rs:211/:217 never got it). Each stratum re-learned the same lesson locally instead of moving it into a shared owner.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:232:- set_keepalive(true) at default OS timing (~2h idle on Linux) is functionally inert yet documented in both copies as preventing idle-stream timeouts (data_plane.rs:101-108, daemon push/data_plane.rs:112-120); the daemon copy silently swallows failure. Misleading comments are a RELIABLE risk: future authors will assume liveness coverage that does not exist.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:246:- [DIVERGE] Bounding the Subscribe/GetState RPC open against a stalled daemon (the 'feedback-server-await-timeouts' lesson)
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:258:- `TRANSFER_STALL_TIMEOUT` = 30s (idle, re-armed on progress) (crates/blit-core/src/remote/transfer/stall_guard.rs:69) — RELIABLE: good — converts infinite stalls into clean TimedOut with explanatory text. FAST/SIMPLE: grpc_fallback.rs:73-80 itself argues hardcoded wall-clock constants violate the adapt-at-runtime principle; the idle (not total) semantics mostly defuse it.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:259:- `GRPC_FALLBACK_CHUNK_BYTES` = 1 MiB (crates/blit-core/src/remote/transfer/grpc_fallback.rs:102) — FAST: deliberately caps fallback frames below the 16-64 MiB TCP tuning so progress is observable at a 100 KB/s floor — trades peak fallback throughput for observability of a watchdog that does not exist yet.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:260:- `CONNECT_TIMEOUT` = 30s (inner connect_timeout + outer total) (crates/blit-app/src/client.rs:24) — SIMPLE: fine (no user knob). Duplication tension: same value re-hardcoded inline at pull.rs:241-242 and push/client/mod.rs:309-310 with no shared constant.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:261:- `(inline) connect_timeout / outer timeout` = 30s + 30s, bare literals (same at push/client/mod.rs:309-310) (crates/blit-core/src/remote/pull.rs:241) — RELIABLE: drift hazard — three copies of one policy; a fix to one (e.g. adding keepalive) will not propagate.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:263:- `DATA_PLANE_ACCEPT_TIMEOUT / DATA_PLANE_TOKEN_TIMEOUT` = 30s / 15s (crates/blit-daemon/src/service/push/data_plane.rs:71) — RELIABLE: correct fix (R46-F7) but the pair is independently re-declared 4× (pull.rs:698-699, pull_sync.rs:597-598, pull_sync.rs:721-722).
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:265:- `PULL_SYNC_ACCEPT_TIMEOUT / PULL_SYNC_TOKEN_TIMEOUT and ACCEPT_TIMEOUT / TOKEN_TIMEOUT` = 30s / 15s, declared twice in one file (also :721-722) (crates/blit-daemon/src/service/pull_sync.rs:597) — RELIABLE: the :716 comment claims reuse of constants 'defined above' while redefining them — documentation already drifted from code within a single file.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:266:- `DNS_RESOLVE_TIMEOUT` = 10s (crates/blit-daemon/src/delegation_gate.rs:266) — none — bounded, clear error text, RELIABLE-aligned.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:267:- `http2_keepalive_interval / http2_keepalive_timeout` = 30s / 20s (server only) (crates/blit-daemon/src/main.rs:138) — RELIABLE: protects only the daemon. The mirror-image client-side setting exists on zero of the three channel builders, so every client stream await trusts a dead peer forever.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:269:- `SNAPSHOT_FETCH_TIMEOUT / SUBSCRIBE_OPEN_TIMEOUT / F1_DISCOVERY_SCAN_TIMEOUT` = 30s / 30s (:5639) / 1500ms (:2515) (crates/blit-tui/src/main.rs:2426) — SIMPLE: fine; duplication: TUI-local re-invention of the open-RPC bound the CLI never got.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:270:- `TarStreamConfig::send_timeout_ms default` = Some(30_000) ms (crates/blit-core/src/tar_stream.rs:38) — SIMPLE: it is an Option config field — a tuning surface, though no caller appears to override it. Yet another independent 30s liveness window.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:271:- `JobsWatchArgs::timeout_secs default` = 0 = wait forever (user flag --timeout-secs) (crates/blit-cli/src/cli.rs:142) — SIMPLE: an explicit user-tuned timeout knob on a tool whose principle is zero tuning; RELIABLE: the default (0) plus no client keepalive means `blit jobs watch` hangs forever on a vanished daemon.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:272:- `set_keepalive(true) with OS-default timing` = true (no interval params; Linux default ≈ 2h idle); second copy daemon push/data_plane.rs:120 with silent `let _ =` (crates/blit-core/src/remote/transfer/data_plane.rs:106) — RELIABLE: comments oversell it ('prevents idle stream timeouts'); at default kernel timing it detects nothing inside any realistic transfer window.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:273:- `(absent) max_decoding_message_size / Endpoint::timeout / client keepalive` = never set on any of the three channel builders (verified by grep: no .timeout(, no tcp_keepalive, no http2_keep_alive on any Endpoint) (crates/blit-core/src/remote/pull.rs:239) — RELIABLE: every post-connect client await is structurally unbounded; tonic's defaults are the de facto liveness policy.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:306:- /home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-app/src/transfers/remote.rs:734 — UNBOUNDED: delegated-pull progress consumer stream.message().await in a loop — CLI waits forever on a stalled destination daemon mid-delegation. RPC open at :698 also unbounded after connect.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:307:- /home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-app/src/transfers/remote.rs:878 — UNBOUNDED: --detach variant waits for the first Started frame with no deadline (RPC open at :843 likewise).
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:308:- /home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-app/src/admin/jobs.rs:26 — UNBOUNDED unary RPCs after bounded connect: get_state (:26-29), cancel_job (:63-67), clear_recent (:111-113), subscribe open — connect_with_timeout bounds only the channel; the RPC await itself has no deadline. Same pattern in admin/ls.rs:74, admin/rm.rs:30, admin/du.rs:48, admin/df.rs:33, admin/find.rs:55, admin/list_modules.rs:32.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:309:- /home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-cli/src/jobs.rs:211 — UNBOUNDED: `jobs watch` subscribe open and GetState (:217) have no outer timeout — the exact gap the TUI fixed with SUBSCRIBE_OPEN_TIMEOUT (audit-8) was not propagated to the CLI.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:327:- The advertised adaptivity is dead code: determine_tuning's warmup branches and analyze_warmup_result (auto_tune/mod.rs:26-67) have no production callers — remote/tuning.rs:13 always passes None, then overwrites the stream counts determine_tuning returned. The 'auto_tune' module name promises runtime adaptation (SIMPLE principle) the system does not perform; all sizing is a static size-keyed table.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:329:- No max_decoding_message_size (or encoding limit) is set anywhere in the workspace (rg over all crates: zero hits). The entire gRPC data path is safe only because every sender independently honors a 1 MiB clamp while tonic's silent 4 MiB default protects receivers. The inert chunk_bytes plumbed into both gRPC sinks (sink.rs:929/1107) invites a future 'fix' that raises the clamp and breaks at 4 MiB with an opaque tonic error — failing neither fast nor plainly (RELIABLE).
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:364:- `CONTROL_PLANE_CHUNK_SIZE` = 1 MiB (/home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-core/src/remote/transfer/data_plane.rs:15) — none in isolation, but tied to GRPC_FALLBACK_CHUNK_BYTES only by comment — RELIABLE risk if either changes
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:365:- `GRPC_FALLBACK_CHUNK_BYTES` = 1 MiB (/home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-core/src/remote/transfer/grpc_fallback.rs:102) — SIMPLE/RELIABLE: well-reasoned (observable cadence at 100 KB/s floor), but it silently relies on tonic's undeclared 4 MiB decode default; no max_decoding_message_size is set anywhere in the workspace, so the safety margin is implicit
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:366:- `RECEIVE_CHUNK_SIZE` = 1 MiB (/home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-core/src/remote/transfer/data_plane.rs:546) — FAST: receive side fixed at 1 MiB while send side uses 16-64 MiB tuned buffers; 2x fresh allocation per streamed file (no pool). The doc justifies 1 MiB vs tokio::io::copy's 8 KiB, not vs the tuned send size
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:367:- `chunk_bytes table in determine_remote_tuning` = 16/32/64 MiB by total_bytes; streams 4-32; tcp_buffer 4/8 MiB; prefetch 16/32 (/home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-core/src/remote/tuning.rs:5) — SIMPLE: static size-keyed table presented as 'auto-tuning'; the actual warmup/bandwidth adaptation path is dead (warmup_result always None). FAST: no feedback from measured throughput
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:368:- `chunk_bytes heuristic in build_plan` = 32 MiB if >1 GB or large-dominated, else 16 MiB (/home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-core/src/transfer_plan.rs:223) — RELIABLE: second independent heuristic disagreeing with remote/tuning.rs (no 64 MiB tier, different threshold); a TCP tuning knob computed inside the transport-agnostic planner
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:369:- `analyze_warmup_result thresholds` = >=6 Gbps: 32 MiB, else 16 MiB (/home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-core/src/auto_tune/mod.rs:26) — SIMPLE: dead code (zero callers) that advertises the adaptive behavior the project promises but does not have
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:371:- `BufferPool formula (client push / daemon pull)` = pool_size = streams*2+4; budget = buffer_size*pool_size*2 (/home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-core/src/remote/push/client/mod.rs:125) — RELIABLE: at max tuning (32 streams x 64 MiB) the budget authorizes ~8.5 GiB and the cache can retain ~4.25 GiB with zero awareness of available memory — OOM risk on small hosts contradicts 'works in every config'
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:372:- `pull_sync pool_size / prefetch` = pool_size = 4; payload_prefetch = 8 (hardcoded, x2 sites) (/home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-daemon/src/service/pull_sync.rs:637) — FAST: ignores tuning.prefetch_count (16/32) that the push path honors — pull-sync prefetch never adapts
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:373:- `TAR_BUFFER_SIZE / TAR_BUFFER_POOL_SIZE` = 4 MiB x 8, no budget (/home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-daemon/src/service/push/data_plane.rs:31) — SIMPLE: third independent pool regime; own doc (620-638) admits it is untuned debt awaiting unification
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:374:- `stream_single_file gRPC buffer` = 64 KiB (bare literal) (/home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-daemon/src/service/pull.rs:602) — FAST: 16x more per-message overhead than every other gRPC path; RELIABLE: invisible to anyone auditing via GRPC_FALLBACK_CHUNK_BYTES
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:375:- `checksum hash buffer` = 256 KiB (x4 here + stack copy at blit-daemon/src/service/pull.rs:524) (/home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-core/src/checksum.rs:148) — SIMPLE: five copy-pasted literals; daemon's stack variant adds a 256 KiB frame on an async-adjacent path
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:377:- `MAX_TAR_SHARD_BYTES` = 256 MiB (/home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-core/src/remote/transfer/tar_safety.rs:50) — FAST/RELIABLE: permits a single 256 MiB read_exact allocation per shard on receive (pipeline.rs:410); intentional single source of truth, correctly referenced by pipeline.rs:335
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:378:- `chunked_copy large-file override` = >1 GiB -> 16 MiB (bypasses BufferSizer) (/home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-core/src/copy/file_copy/chunked.rs:35) — SIMPLE: hardcoded special case that overrides the supposed sizing authority for exactly the files where sizing matters most
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:379:- `MAX_SPLICE` = 8 MiB (/home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-core/src/zero_copy.rs:69) — none observed; separate kernel-path regime
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:381:- `DEFAULT_PAYLOAD_PREFETCH` = 8 (/home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-core/src/remote/transfer/payload.rs:108) — SIMPLE: prefetch decided three ways — this constant (daemon pull-sync, orchestrator), tuning.prefetch_count (client push), and bare literal 8 (pull_sync from_stream)
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:382:- `data-plane chunk floor` = 64 KiB (.max(64*1024)), repeated at line 584 and in 4 pool constructions (/home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-core/src/remote/transfer/data_plane.rs:66) — SIMPLE: six copies of the same floor with no named constant
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:414:- /home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-core/src/zero_copy.rs:69 — constant (kernel path) — MAX_SPLICE = 8 MiB per splice call — separate sizing regime for the zero-copy path.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:438:- --retry/--wait is silently inert on the primary gRPC paths. Every tonic::Status->String conversion (pull.rs:307/332/493/507/541, format_status at pull.rs:116 feeding PullSyncError at 792-798, push helpers.rs:48, the entire delegated path in blit-app/remote.rs and daemon delegated_pull.rs:216/245) destroys the io::Error chain that is_retryable (retry.rs:27-33) requires. The code knows: TODOs at pull.rs:322-329 and 780-788 literally say '--retry/--wait will silently stop working on this code path' — a RELIABLE failure mode where the advertised flag does nothing and tells no one. audit-h3c slice 2, which would fix it, is unshipped (stall_guard.rs:35-41).
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:465:- `TRANSFER_STALL_TIMEOUT` = Duration::from_secs(30) (/home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-core/src/remote/transfer/stall_guard.rs:69) — SIMPLE: zero-tuning, owner-decided, idle-not-total semantics — good. But grpc_fallback.rs:73-80 itself declares hardcoded wall-clock constants a violation of the FAST principle and promises a progress-cadence-derived policy for slice 2; the 30s figure is exactly such a magic number, just a defensible one.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:466:- `--retry default` = 0 (retries disabled) (/home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-cli/src/cli.rs:275) — RELIABLE vs SIMPLE: the tool does not adapt — resilience is opt-in user tuning, contradicting 'zero user tuning, adapts at runtime'. A transient network drop fails the run unless the user predicted it.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:467:- `--wait default` = 5 (seconds, fixed interval) (/home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-cli/src/cli.rs:283) — SIMPLE: fixed linear wait, no exponential backoff or jitter; user must tune it. Also duplicated as literal 'wait: 5' in test fixtures (blit-cli/src/transfers/mod.rs:706,755,818).
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:471:- `PULL_ACCEPT_TIMEOUT / PULL_TOKEN_TIMEOUT` = 30s / 15s (/home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-daemon/src/service/pull.rs:698) — Function-local consts, not co-located with TRANSFER_STALL_TIMEOUT despite stall_guard.rs:31 and :65 documenting them as part of the same coverage story. Hardcoded but bounded-failure-is-better-than-hang, so RELIABLE-positive.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:472:- `GRPC_FALLBACK_CHUNK_BYTES` = 1 MiB (/home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-core/src/remote/transfer/grpc_fallback.rs:102) — FAST: deliberately caps gRPC fallback frames far below the 16-64 MiB TCP tuning so a future watchdog can observe cadence — trades throughput on the fallback path for observability that doesn't exist yet (slice 2 unshipped), so today it's all cost, no watchdog.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:473:- `DEFAULT_BLOCK_SIZE / MAX_BLOCK_SIZE` = 1 MiB / 64 MiB (MAX at line 19) (/home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-core/src/copy/file_copy/resume.rs:16) — SIMPLE-positive (client sends block_size:0 = 'use default' at blit-cli/src/transfers/remote.rs:383); daemon clamps to MAX at pull_sync.rs:84. Fixed 1 MiB granularity is never adapted to file size or link speed (mild FAST tension).
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:491:- /home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-core/src/remote/pull.rs:493 — CLASSIFIER-BLIND: scan_remote_files (493, 507 with TODO at 502-504) and open_remote_file (541) strip Status to String the same way
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:514:No module owns error handling; ownership is smeared across all six crates plus the proto. The nominal owner, blit_core::errors (TransferError/ErrorCategory/categorize_io_error, errors.rs:12-121, exported at lib.rs:9), is dead code with zero consumers, while the live retry policy lives in blit-app/src/transfers/retry.rs with an io::ErrorKind table that directly contradicts the dead one on three kinds (ConnectionRefused, UnexpectedEof, NotConnected). The workspace is uniformly eyre (thiserror and anyhow appear nowhere), but at least seven distinct styles coexist: chain-preserving context, chain-destroying eyre! stringification, three hand-rolled typed errors, tonic::Status-as-error with 116/189 constructors collapsed to Status::internal and line-dependent {err} vs {err:#} formatting, sentinel io::Errors for retry signaling, proto-carried string errors, and five different stderr prefixes. The sharpest problem is a self-documented broken contract: stall_guard.rs manufactures downcastable TimedOut errors precisely so --retry/--wait can classify stalls as retryable, but PullSyncError/format_status (pull.rs:82/116) and the dominant eyre!(status.message().to_string()) pattern (15+ sites) flatten errors to strings before the classifier sees them — the TODO at pull.rs:780-788 admits retry silently no-ops on the pull-sync path. Secondary: the same stringification drops the gRPC code, so a code-only Status renders as an empty user-facing error, violating RELIABLE's plain-failure principle.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:518:- --retry/--wait is silently broken on the pull-sync path: stall_guard manufactures io::ErrorKind::TimedOut precisely so retry.rs:27 can downcast it, but PullSyncError (pull.rs:82) and format_status (pull.rs:116) flatten the Status to a String before it reaches the eyre chain. The repo knows: TODO(audit-h3c-2) at pull.rs:780-788 documents the break, unfixed. RELIABLE violation — the feature degrades with no error at all.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:550:- `CONNECT_TIMEOUT` = Duration::from_secs(30) (/home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-app/src/client.rs:24) — SIMPLE: fixed, no user tuning needed — fine. RELIABLE tension: it is the named, documented copy of a value that also exists as four anonymous literals in blit-core; a future change here will not propagate.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:551:- `(unnamed connect timeout, pull)` = std::time::Duration::from_secs(30) (also :242) (/home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-core/src/remote/pull.rs:241) — RELIABLE: duplicate of CONNECT_TIMEOUT as a bare literal; its timeout error text ('connecting to {} timed out', :244) omits the duration, so the user is not told how long blit waited.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:553:- `TRANSFER_STALL_TIMEOUT` = Duration::from_secs(30) (/home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-core/src/remote/transfer/stall_guard.rs:69) — SIMPLE: good — adaptive watchdog re-armed on progress, no tuning knob. RELIABLE tension: its entire purpose (a downcastable TimedOut for --retry) is voided on the pull-sync path by PullSyncError stringification (pull.rs:780 TODO).
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:570:- /home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-core/src/remote/transfer/sink.rs:985 — 11 map_err sites collapsing mpsc SendError into fixed strings: "gRPC channel closed" (:985,:1016,:1038,:1048,:1056,:1080) and "gRPC pull stream closed" (:1156,:1187,:1209,:1219,:1227) — no path/file context, not explanatory per RELIABLE.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:575:- /home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-daemon/src/service/pull.rs:516 — io::Error→Status::internal(format!("open {}: {}")) — file-level NotFound/PermissionDenied become code INTERNAL on the wire. Daemon-wide tally: 116 of 189 Status constructors are Status::internal (40 invalid_argument, 13 permission_denied, 8 failed_precondition, 7 not_found, 5 aborted), so the gRPC code dimension carries almost no information for clients.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:577:- /home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-app/src/transfers/remote.rs:698 — Best user-facing failure text in the repo: branches on Code::Unimplemented/Unavailable and appends remediation ("upgrade the destination daemon", "pass --relay-via-cli") at :698-726 and :737-751; repeated for relay path at :844-908. Exemplary RELIABLE, but isolated to delegation.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:594:No module owns progress reporting. blit-core/src/remote/transfer/progress.rs owns the client-side types (ProgressEvent, RemoteTransferProgress, ByteProgressSink) but not their semantics: five producer families assign three incompatible meanings to the same enum (pull-gRPC: bytes on Payload, FileComplete carries 0; TCP receive: the same bytes on BOTH Payload and FileComplete; push send: bytes on FileComplete only, no Payload), and consumers cope divergently — the TUI carries three per-direction folding rules (progress_accum.rs) while the CLI uses one rule that double-counts bytes on TCP pulls (transfers/remote.rs:64-76 vs pipeline.rs:233-235). End to end there are four live pipelines (client ProgressEvent → CLI 1 Hz monitor / TUI accumulators; daemon ActiveJobs atomic → 10 Hz TransferProgress broadcast → jobs watch / TUI; DelegatedPullProgress wire stream; local indicatif spinner) plus two dead ones (proto BytesProgress, which has consumers and tests but no emitter, and the local LocalMirrorOptions.progress / chunked_copy_file ProgressBar plumbing, which has writers but no readers). They disagree on cadence (100 ms vs 1 s vs per-event vs never), units (binary MiB vs decimal KB/MB/GB vs raw bytes), denominators (enumerated manifest vs need-list vs post-hoc transferred count), and JSON schema/stream. Sharpest problems: the CLI's user-facing byte count is ~2x wrong on TCP pulls and contradicts the summary printed immediately after; delegated transfers and most daemon-event subscribers see zeros for the entire transfer because only delegated_pull wires the byte counter and the daemon never emits BytesProgress; and heartbeats exist only as reserved proto fields.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:598:- CLI byte counts are ~2x on TCP pulls: pipeline.rs:233-235 emits Payload(0,bytes) AND FileComplete(path,bytes) for each received file; the CLI monitor (transfers/remote.rs:64-76) adds bytes from both, so the live '[progress] ... MiB copied' and the final '[progress] final: ... MiB total' lines double-count, then the very next 'Pull complete: N bytes' summary (from the wire report) contradicts them. The TUI documented and dodged exactly this trap (progress_accum.rs:12-20); the CLI never got the fix. RELIABLE violation with user-visible self-contradiction.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:601:- ProgressEvent has no semantic contract: three producer families assign three incompatible meanings to Payload/FileComplete bytes (chunk-delta vs whole-file vs zero), forcing consumers to know which transfer direction they are watching. Any new producer or consumer (e.g. the planned AppProgressEvent reshape referenced at blit-app remote.rs:22,663) must rediscover this folklore; the CLI already got it wrong.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:636:- `DEFAULT_PROGRESS_TICK_MS` = 100 (10 Hz) (/home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-daemon/src/service/core.rs:61) — SIMPLE: fixed, no tuning — good. FAST: tick_progress_emit holds the ActiveJobs table mutex and broadcasts one event per active row per tick even for rows whose counter is permanently 0 (push/pull/pull_sync), pure wasted wire/CPU today.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:637:- `SUBSCRIBE_BROADCAST_CAPACITY` = 256 events (/home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-daemon/src/service/core.rs:41) — RELIABLE: at 10 Hz × N transfers, 256 is ~25 s of headroom for 1 transfer but ~2.5 s for 10; a lagging subscriber gets Status::aborted and must re-subscribe — capacity is load-dependent but fixed.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:638:- `SUBSCRIBE_MPSC_CAPACITY` = 64 events (/home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-daemon/src/service/core.rs:53) — RELIABLE: per-subscriber buffer sized to 'one or two tick intervals' by comment — only true for small active-row counts.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:639:- `JOB_EVENT_RING_CAP` = 64 events per row (/home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-daemon/src/active_jobs.rs:114) — SIMPLE: at 10 Hz the replay_recent ring holds only ~6.4 s of history; comment says 'a few seconds is plenty' — adapts to nothing.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:641:- `spinner steady tick` = 120 ms (/home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-cli/src/transfers/local.rs:107) — RELIABLE: the local-transfer spinner is pure animation — fed by no byte counter, it spins identically whether the transfer is moving or hung, violating 'fails plainly'.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:643:- `format_bps decimal thresholds` = 1_000 / 1_000_000 / 1_000_000_000 (decimal KB/MB/GB) (/home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-cli/src/jobs.rs:472) — RELIABLE/SIMPLE: disagrees with the binary MiB units at transfers/remote.rs:104-110; same tool, two unit systems.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:644:- `TransferProgress zero placeholders` = bytes_total=0, files_completed=0, files_total=0 (/home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-daemon/src/service/core.rs:240) — RELIABLE: hardcoded zeros shipped on every event; consumers cannot distinguish 'unknown' from 'zero', and no percent/ETA is possible anywhere in the system.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:694:No single module owns transfer tuning. The nominal owner — blit_core::remote::tuning + auto_tune — is itself a set of frozen byte-total ladders whose genuinely adaptive half (bandwidth warmup) is dead code with zero production callers, and whose output fields are honored fully by exactly one path (push client): the live pull-sync path computes the tuning and then discards everything except chunk_bytes, running single-stream with a hardcoded pool of 4. Around that hollow center, at least four independent chunk-size ladders, two disagreeing stream-count ladders, three buffer-pool sizing formulas, three small/medium/large taxonomies, and four declarations of the same 30s connect deadline have accreted — clear strata from different authoring passes that agree only where literals happen to coincide. The sharpest problems are (1) the invisible tonic 4 MiB decode limit nowhere overridden while the daemon's 500-entry manifest batches can legally exceed it, (2) push and pull using different frozen parallelism ladders for identical workloads, and (3) the 64 MiB tuning ceiling equaling the wire-side MAX_WIRE_BLOCK_BYTES by parallel declaration, so the obvious 'tune it up' edit bricks remote transfers. The only true closed loop (derive_local_plan_tuning over perf history) covers the local planner alone; the network path — the one the FAST/SIMPLE principles most target — adapts to nothing measured at runtime.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:698:- Invisible 4 MiB ceiling: no max_decoding_message_size/max_encoding_message_size anywhere in the workspace (verified by direct search), so every gRPC stream runs on tonic's default 4 MiB decode cap. The pull manifest path batches ENUM_BATCH_SIZE=500 FileHeaders per message (blit-daemon/src/service/pull.rs:31,227) while the wire validator elsewhere accepts paths to 64 KiB (pipeline.rs:325) — 500 long-pathed entries can exceed 4 MiB and abort the transfer with a generic tonic status, violating RELIABLE (fails, but not plainly) on deep/long-path trees.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:699:- The adaptive mechanism is open-loop for remote transfers: determine_tuning's bandwidth-aware branches and analyze_warmup_result are dead code (only caller is tuning.rs:13 with warmup=None; analyze_warmup_result has zero callers). Every remote tuning decision reduces to frozen byte-total ladders, so SIMPLE ('adapts at runtime') is true only for the local planner (derive_local_plan_tuning + perf_history) — the network path never learns.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:700:- Push and pull disagree on parallelism for identical workloads: tuning.rs:14-26 (push: up to 24/32 streams) vs pull_stream_count at daemon pull.rs:915 (max 16, different per-tier values). Two AI strata each froze their own ladder; FAST is inconsistent by direction of transfer.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:704:- Server-side receive concurrency frozen at MAX_PARALLEL_TAR_TASKS=4 (daemon push/data_plane.rs:28) regardless of core count, while clients tune up to 32 send streams — the receive side is a hardcoded bottleneck that caps FAST on big-core servers.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:742:- `determine_remote_tuning ladders (chunk/streams/bufs/prefetch)` = chunk 16/32/64 MiB; streams (4,8)..(24,32); tcp_buffer 4/8 MiB; prefetch 16/32 — keyed on total_bytes only (crates/blit-core/src/remote/tuning.rs:5) — SIMPLE: this IS the sanctioned mechanism, yet it adapts only to workload size, never to measured bandwidth, RTT, or core count — all breakpoints frozen at authoring. FAST: a 1 GbE link and a 100 GbE link with the same 10 GiB tree get identical stream/chunk choices.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:743:- `determine_tuning warmup branches` = gbps>8→6 streams/8 MiB/32; >3→4/4 MiB/16; else 2/1 MiB/8; max_streams always 8 (crates/blit-core/src/auto_tune/mod.rs:45) — SIMPLE/FAST: dead adaptive path — warmup_result is None at the only production call (tuning.rs:13), and tuning.rs then overwrites initial/max_streams anyway. The runtime-adaptation story exists in code but is disconnected. Also its max_streams=8 contradicts tuning.rs's up-to-32.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:744:- `TRANSFER_STALL_TIMEOUT` = 30s of zero byte progress (crates/blit-core/src/remote/transfer/stall_guard.rs:69) — RELIABLE: good fail-plainly design and properly shared; value itself is structural-leaning. Minor: not scaled to link class, but byte-level progress observation makes 30s safe even on slow links.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:745:- `CONNECT_TIMEOUT (x4 declarations)` = 30s (also inline at pull.rs:241-242, push/client/mod.rs:309-310, delegated_pull.rs:35) (crates/blit-app/src/client.rs:24) — RELIABLE: the bound itself is good (fail in 30s, not OS 60-127s). SIMPLE-of-code: four declarations of one decision; three are inline literals.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:746:- `tonic decode limit (ABSENT)` = implicit 4 MiB tonic default — zero hits for max_decoding_message_size/max_encoding_message_size workspace-wide (crates/blit-core/src/remote/pull.rs:239) — RELIABLE: an invisible constant nobody chose. Pull manifest batches (ENUM_BATCH_SIZE=500 FileHeaders, paths legal up to MAX_WIRE_PATH_LEN=64 KiB) can exceed 4 MiB and kill the stream with a generic tonic error — failure neither fast nor plain.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:747:- `http2_keepalive interval/timeout (server only)` = 30s interval / 20s timeout (crates/blit-daemon/src/main.rs:138) — RELIABLE: clients have no counterpart, so a daemon that dies silently mid-control-stream is detected only by the data-plane stall guard or OS timeout; gRPC-fallback transfers (no TCP data plane) lean entirely on tonic defaults.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:748:- `pull_stream_count ladder` = 1/2/4/8/10/12/16 streams at 32 MiB..32 GiB breakpoints (crates/blit-daemon/src/service/pull.rs:915) — FAST+SIMPLE: second frozen ladder disagreeing with tuning.rs — pull is capped at 16 streams where push tunes to 24-32; neither measures the link.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:749:- `pull_sync pool_size + prefetch` = pool_size=4, prefetch literal 8, single stream (crates/blit-daemon/src/service/pull_sync.rs:637) — FAST: the live pull path discards tuning.initial_streams/max_streams/prefetch_count/tcp_buffer_size — large pulls run one TCP stream with a 4-buffer pool regardless of hardware.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:750:- `MAX_PARALLEL_TAR_TASKS / TAR_BUFFER_SIZE / TAR_BUFFER_POOL_SIZE` = 4 tasks / 4 MiB / 8 buffers (crates/blit-daemon/src/service/push/data_plane.rs:28) — FAST: server-side untar concurrency fixed at 4 regardless of cores while the client may push up to 32 streams — receive side is a frozen bottleneck.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:753:- `MAX_WIRE_BLOCK_BYTES` = 64 MiB (crates/blit-core/src/remote/transfer/pipeline.rs:339) — RELIABLE: structural anti-OOM cap, but equals tuning's max chunk exactly via parallel declaration — a tuning bump without this file is a remote-transfer outage.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:756:- `DEFAULT_PAYLOAD_PREFETCH` = 8 (crates/blit-core/src/remote/transfer/payload.rs:108) — SIMPLE: fallback pipeline depth frozen; tuning.prefetch_count exists but only kicks in >=512 MiB and only the push client honors it.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:757:- `BufferSizer curve` = min 1 MiB, max 16 MiB, base 4/8 MiB, tiers at 10 MB/100 MB, 900 MiB scale span, 10% memory cap, 8 KiB floor (crates/blit-core/src/buffer.rs:27) — SIMPLE: half-adaptive — memory cap reads real RAM, but the curve and the 16 MiB ceiling are authoring-time guesses ('for 25GbE saturation' per module doc, never re-measured).
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:758:- `chunked_copy 1 GiB→16 MiB branch` = file_size > 1_073_741_824 → 16 MiB fixed (crates/blit-core/src/copy/file_copy/chunked.rs:35) — SIMPLE: bypasses BufferSizer (including its memory cap) for the biggest files with a frozen value that happens to equal BufferSizer's max — redundant divergent decision.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:759:- `Windows no-buffering thresholds` = threshold 1 GiB, floor 2 GiB, headroom 512 MiB, small-file max 512 MiB (crates/blit-core/src/copy/windows.rs:20) — SIMPLE: platform cache-bypass policy frozen at authoring; partially memory-aware via floor/headroom.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:760:- `TINY/HUGE fast-path thresholds` = 256 files / 256 MiB total / 1 GiB single (crates/blit-core/src/orchestrator/fast_path.rs:11) — SIMPLE: routing tuned by authoring intuition; sits beside a perf-history system that could inform it but doesn't.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:762:- `ENUM_BATCH_SIZE / MIN_BYTES_FOR_TUNING` = 500 files/batch; 16 MiB before data-plane start (crates/blit-daemon/src/service/pull.rs:31) — RELIABLE: 500-entry batches are the plausible 4 MiB-decode-limit violator; SIMPLE: both frozen guesses.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:763:- `FILE_LIST batch/early-flush set` = 16Ki entries/512 KiB/25 ms; 128/64 KiB/5 ms; channel 256Ki (crates/blit-daemon/src/service/push/control.rs:25) — SIMPLE: six interlocking frozen micro-tuning values; FILE_UPLOAD_CHANNEL_CAPACITY=262,144 entries is a large fixed memory commitment.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:764:- `transfer_plan shard/bundle targets` = shard 4/8/32/64 MiB, count 256/1024/2048 clamp 128-4096; bundle 128/256/384 MiB, spread 1.25x (crates/blit-core/src/transfer_plan.rs:138) — SIMPLE: dense authoring-time heuristic cluster — though this is the one cluster the closed-loop local tuner can override via PlanOptions.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:765:- `categorize_files limits` = small <1 MiB, medium <100 MB (crates/blit-core/src/fs_enum.rs:499) — SIMPLE: third competing size taxonomy in the workspace.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:767:- `accept/token timeout pair (x4)` = 30s accept / 15s token (crates/blit-daemon/src/service/push/data_plane.rs:71) — RELIABLE-positive (bounded waits) but quadruplicated; none on values.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:768:- `TarConfig defaults` = 64-chunk channel, 1 MiB chunks, 30s send timeout (crates/blit-core/src/tar_stream.rs:36) — SIMPLE: frozen; consistent with the 1 MiB family.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:769:- `MAX_SPLICE` = 8 MiB (crates/blit-core/src/zero_copy.rs:69) — none — structural syscall batching.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:770:- `retry/wait CLI defaults` = retry=0, wait=5s, fixed spacing (run_with_retries, blit-app/src/transfers/retry.rs:55) (crates/blit-cli/src/cli.rs:278) — SIMPLE: an explicit user knob in a zero-tuning tool (owner-approved robocopy parity); no backoff — fixed 5s spacing is a frozen recovery policy.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:777:- /home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-core/src/remote/tuning.rs:4 — Sanctioned owner (nominal): determine_remote_tuning — size-based ladders for chunk_bytes (16/32/64 MiB at 512MiB/8GiB breakpoints), initial/max streams (4..32 across 6 byte tiers), tcp_buffer_size (4/8 MiB), prefetch (16/32) — Pure open-loop: input is only total_bytes. Calls determine_tuning(default, None) at line 13 — the warmup parameter is ALWAYS None.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:802:- /home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-core/src/zero_copy.rs:69 — MAX_SPLICE=8 MiB per splice/copy_file_range call — Structural syscall batching; benign.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:810:- /home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-app/src/transfers/retry.rs:55 — run_with_retries — retry count/wait come from CLI flags (default retry=0, wait=5s per blit-cli/src/cli.rs:633-639); fixed (non-backoff) spacing — User-tunable knob (mild SIMPLE tension, owner-approved robocopy parity); constant wait rather than exponential backoff.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:825:- Long-path support is decorative: win_fs::ensure_long_path (win_fs.rs:229) is called only by fs_capability/windows.rs:172 for volume probing. Every actual I/O path — CopyFileExW in copy/windows.rs:344-363 and fs_capability/windows.rs:234-247, all std OpenOptions/File::create in file_copy/, the sinks, enumeration, and every delete loop — passes raw paths. Trees deeper than 260 chars fail on stock Windows with raw OS errors, violating RELIABLE ('fails plainly'), and the one helper built to fix it is orphaned.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:828:- The wire permission model is Unix-only and fails silently: proto FileHeader.permissions (blit.proto:192) carries Unix mode bits; Windows sources send 0 (helpers.rs:78, util.rs:145), Windows destinations discard (sink.rs:431-435, 733-735, tar_safety.rs:245). The Windows read-only attribute survives only LOCAL copies (fs_capability/windows.rs:85-94). No warning, no docs flag — silent metadata loss contradicts RELIABLE.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:832:- cached_probe (fs_capability/mod.rs:96-117) caches per-device only under cfg(unix); the cfg(not(unix)) branch re-runs probe_capabilities — and volume_info_for_path's GetVolumePathNameW/GetVolumeInformationW — on every call. FAST tension on exactly the platform with the slowest filesystem metadata calls (partially mitigated by the separate BLOCK_CLONE_CACHE in fs_capability/windows.rs:31).
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:834:- zero_copy.rs is a fully dead Linux-only module (zero callers; daemon bytes_zero_copy stats are constant 0) — strata debris that misleads readers into thinking splice-based zero-copy is wired into the TCP data plane.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:868:- `WINDOWS_NO_BUFFERING_THRESHOLD` = 1 GiB (/home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-core/src/copy/windows.rs:20) — FAST/SIMPLE: the no-buffering decision adapts to RAM (good SIMPLE) but the file-size cliffs are fixed regardless of storage medium; well unit-tested though
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:869:- `WINDOWS_NO_BUFFERING_FLOOR` = 2 GiB (/home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-core/src/copy/windows.rs:21) — FAST: static floor when GlobalMemoryStatusEx fails; arbitrary on 256 GB-RAM servers
