docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:870:- `WINDOWS_NO_BUFFERING_HEADROOM` = 512 MiB (/home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-core/src/copy/windows.rs:22) — FAST: fixed cache headroom independent of total RAM
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:872:- `COPY_FILE_NO_BUFFERING_FLAG` = 0x0000_1000 (/home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-core/src/copy/windows.rs:24) — RELIABLE (minor): hand-rolled instead of the windows-crate COPY_FILE_NO_BUFFERING constant
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:873:- `FILE_FLAG_SEQUENTIAL_SCAN` = 0x0800_0000 (/home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-core/src/copy/file_copy/mod.rs:22) — RELIABLE (minor): hand-rolled Win32 constant; also applied to the destination open at mod.rs:103 where a write-mostly handle gains nothing from a read-ahead hint
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:874:- `FSCTL_DUPLICATE_EXTENTS_TO_FILE` = ctl_code(0x9, 623, 0, FILE_WRITE_ACCESS) (/home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-core/src/copy/windows.rs:194) — RELIABLE: hand-computed ioctl code instead of the typed windows-crate constant; silent if wrong
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:875:- `zero_threshold (sparse zero-run seek)` = 256 KiB (zero_buf 64 KiB) (/home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-core/src/copy/file_copy/clone.rs:234) — FAST: Windows sparse heuristic is a byte-scan + threshold while the Unix path uses SEEK_HOLE — different hole-detection fidelity per OS, neither configurable (consistent with SIMPLE)
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:876:- `chunked large-file chunk size` = >1 GiB → 16 MiB, else BufferSizer (/home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-core/src/copy/file_copy/chunked.rs:35) — FAST: bypasses the adaptive BufferSizer above an arbitrary 1 GiB cutoff
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:877:- `volume_path_buf / fs_name_buf sizes` = 512 u16 / 64 u16 (/home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-core/src/fs_capability/windows.rs:175) — RELIABLE (minor): fixed buffers for GetVolumePathNameW/GetVolumeInformationW; extreme long-path volumes could truncate, error path then misattributes
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:878:- `CasefoldKey fold algorithm` = to_ascii_lowercase() (/home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-core/src/mirror_planner.rs:40) — RELIABLE: ASCII-only fold under-approximates NTFS Unicode case-insensitivity — non-ASCII case pairs look 'extraneous' to mirror deletion on Windows; and macOS case-insensitive APFS gets no fold at all
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:879:- `strip prefix literal` = r"\\?\" (also admin.rs:619) (/home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-app/src/diagnostics/dump.rs:171) — RELIABLE: misses the \\?\UNC\ canonical form, so df/du mount matching silently fails for UNC destinations in two places
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:913:- /home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-core/src/zero_copy.rs:3 — dead Linux-only module — splice_from_socket_to_file etc., all cfg(all(unix, not(macos))); zero callers anywhere in the workspace; daemon bytes_zero_copy stats fields are always 0/pass-through
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:918:- /home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-app/src/transfers/remote.rs:203 — client-side pull-mirror purge, Windows-blind — delete_listed_paths: remove_file (235) with no readonly clearing; hard-errors on failure (good RELIABLE) but on Windows will error on read-only files that local mirror would have deleted
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:920:- /home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-app/src/diagnostics/dump.rs:71 — diagnostics parity gap — same_device: cfg(unix) dev() comparison; cfg(not(unix)) returns None — reflink/zero-copy predictor input absent on Windows
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:939:- Orphaned daemon pull data plane: stream_pull_streaming (service/pull.rs:297) holds a bare JoinHandle; when the client cancels the gRPC stream, the handler errors on its next tx.send (e.g. pull.rs:325 manifest batch) and the `?` return DROPS the handle, detaching the TCP data plane mid-transfer. There is no StallGuard on the daemon's send side; the orphan runs until a TCP error/reset, or indefinitely if the client process keeps the data socket open while abandoning the control stream — unreachable by CancelJob (Pull is Unsupported) and invisible cleanup-wise. Same pattern in push/control.rs:57.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:940:- blitd has no shutdown handling at all (main.rs:137-142): no ctrl_c, no serve_with_shutdown, while the far less critical prometheus bridge does (server.rs:114-121). Operator Ctrl-C aborts in-flight file writes and the recents writer with no drain and no explanatory output — fails, but not plainly (RELIABLE violation).
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:967:- `TRANSFER_STALL_TIMEOUT` = Duration::from_secs(30) (/home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-core/src/remote/transfer/stall_guard.rs:69) — SIMPLE: no user knob (good). RELIABLE: a legitimately quiet link (laptop sleep, transient route flap >30s) hard-fails the transfer with TimedOut; it is also the de-facto bound on how long an orphaned (detached, uncancelled) receive task lives — but only on read paths, not the daemon pull SEND path.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:968:- `PULL_ACCEPT_TIMEOUT / PULL_TOKEN_TIMEOUT` = 30s / 15s (/home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-daemon/src/service/pull.rs:698) — RELIABLE: bounds a peer that opens the control RPC but never the data socket (R47-F5). Tension is the 4x duplication (see duplicates), not the values.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:969:- `PULL_SYNC_ACCEPT_TIMEOUT / PULL_SYNC_TOKEN_TIMEOUT (plus a second anonymous ACCEPT_TIMEOUT/TOKEN_TIMEOUT pair)` = 30s / 15s (duplicated again at 719-720) (/home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-daemon/src/service/pull_sync.rs:597) — two const pairs in one file for the same concept — pure drift hazard against SIMPLE/RELIABLE
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:971:- `http2_keepalive_interval / http2_keepalive_timeout (daemon server)` = 30s / 20s (/home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-daemon/src/main.rs:138) — RELIABLE: this is what makes tx.closed()-based cancellation fire for silently-dead peers. Configured ONLY on the server — none of the three client channel builders set keepalive, so a daemon-side hang is detected by clients only via OS TCP timeouts (matches the seed finding).
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:972:- `mpsc::channel(32) response buffers at every dispatch site` = 32 (also 558, 712) (/home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-daemon/src/service/core.rs:463) — FAST/RELIABLE: tx.closed() only resolves when the Receiver is dropped; buffered sends succeed after disconnect until tonic drops rx, slightly delaying disconnect detection on the non-raced push/pull/pull_sync paths
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:983:- /home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-daemon/src/service/core.rs:1093 — CancelJob RPC handler — maps CancelOutcome to OK/FailedPrecondition/NotFound/PermissionDenied with plain explanatory text — good RELIABLE posture
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:998:- /home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-daemon/src/service/push/data_plane.rs:841 — StallGuard on push receive socket (audit-h3a) — bounds a peer that opens the socket then stalls
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:1016:blit-core is five recognizable strata stacked without demolition. (1) An Oct-2025 foundation layer: synchronous std::fs local-copy engine, test_-prefixed tests, aspirational '25GbE/rclone-inspired' docs, and vestiges of an even earlier 'Windows focus' restart (tar_stream.rs cites the deleted streaming_batch.rs). (2) An Oct/Nov-2025 remote v1 layer: two parallel hand-rolled gRPC monoliths (pull.rs 2212 lines, push/client 1133 lines) that duplicate each other's channel builder verbatim and tune via static byte-size ladders because the planned warmup probe was never built. (3) A small Jan-2026 design-doc restart (manifest.rs lived, errors.rs is dead on arrival). (4) An Apr/May-2026 pipeline unification (TransferSource/TransferSink/diff_planner) that is explicitly unfinished — its own docs say push/pull adopt it in 'step 3b and step 4', the unified FsTransferSource still calls push-era helpers, and re-export shims mark the move. (5) A May/Jun-2026 review-loop layer (~230 R##-F#/audit-* comment references) that added safety chokepoints (path_safety, path_posix, tar_safety, stall_guard) explicitly to replace 'half-a-dozen ad-hoc validators that drifted apart'. The local-copy and remote sides barely share idioms: sync+rayon vs async+tokio, NoopLogger vs ProgressEvent, four parallel comparison-mode enums with mapping shims in three crates. Dead weight is substantial: five entire modules (errors, tar_stream, zero_copy, delete, copy/parallel+stats) plus chunked_copy_file, the warmup machinery, transfer_payloads_via_control_plane, the deprecated Pull client path, and several fs_enum helpers have zero callers. Transport policy confirms the seed findings: chunk_bytes is a TCP knob threaded through transport-agnostic types and inert in the gRPC sinks (clamped to a 1 MiB constant), no max_decoding_message_size anywhere (4 MiB tonic default), and client channels set no HTTP/2 keepalive while the daemon server does — symptoms of the three independent channel builders. Searches run for auditability: per-symbol rg over crates/ and tests/ for every suspect type/function named above; module-path greps (mod::, pub use tracing); style greps (eyre/anyhow counts, finding-ID density, test_-prefix counts, box-drawing headers, #[tokio::test] counts); transport greps (keepalive, max_decoding_message_size, tcp_nodelay, connect_timeout); and git log --follow --diff-filter=A dating of every module cited.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:1028:- **Remote push/pull v1 stratum (added 2025-10-19 to 2025-11-21): hand-rolled gRPC control-plane event loops with 12+ mutable state flags, `eyre!(status.message().to_string())` error mapping that strips status codes, hard-coded byte-size threshold ladders, grab-bag helpers.rs modules** — The first remote-transfer author(s). Push and pull were written as two parallel monoliths that independently duplicate connection setup, path normalization, and payload streaming. Tuning is a static if-ladder on total_bytes (not runtime-adaptive, in tension with the SIMPLE/'adapts at runtime' principle since the warmup probe that would feed it was never built). The two largest files in the crate (pull.rs 2212 lines, push/client/mod.rs 1133 lines) are this stratum's event loops, later heavily annotated by the audit stratum.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:1073:- src/zero_copy.rs — entire module (219 lines: splice_from_socket_to_file, ZeroCopyResult, AsRawFileDescriptor)
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:1074:  - evidence: rg 'zero_copy::|splice_from_socket_to_file|ZeroCopyResult|AsRawFileDescriptor' across crates/ and tests/ matches only lib.rs:27 and the file itself. The `bytes_zero_copy` fields elsewhere (e.g. blit-daemon/src/service/push/data_plane.rs:39) are proto summary counters, hardcoded to 0 at most sites — unrelated to this module. Added 2025-10-17, last touched 2025-10-18.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:1075:  - status: Abandoned aspirational splice/zero-copy support that the receive pipeline never adopted (receive uses receive_stream_double_buffered userspace copies). Either revive for the FAST principle or delete; today it is misleading dead weight.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:1087:  - status: Abandoned design: a warmup probe was planned (module doc auto_tune/mod.rs:1-4 'Provides warmup probes') but never built, leaving static heuristics — directly at odds with the SIMPLE 'adapts at runtime' principle. The newer derive_local_plan_tuning half of the same file (history-driven, R56-era) is the live successor for local transfers only.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:1106:blit-daemon is four readable geological layers. (1) An original transfer-engine stratum (pull.rs, pull_sync.rs, push/*) with terse phase comments, per-file `eprintln!` hot-path logging under inconsistent bracket tags, and three hand-rolled stream-count ladders that re-derive (and then clamp against) blit-core's tuning. (2) A review-remediation stratum that patched R-/audit-/F-numbered fixes into those files four separate times instead of extracting shared code — the accept+token handshake exists in 4 copies with 3 sets of identical timeout constants (the `StdDuration`/`StdDuration2` aliases in pull_sync.rs are the smoking gun), and only the push copy sets TCP nodelay/keepalive. (3) A jobs/TUI milestone stratum (active_jobs.rs, core.rs Subscribe/GetState machinery, recents_store.rs, metrics.rs) written in slice-IDs (b-1..c-5b, m-jobs, rec) whose `#[allow(dead_code)] // lands in next slice` annotations were never swept after the consumers landed. (4) A newest delegation stratum (delegated_pull.rs, delegation_gate.rs, net_timeout.rs) with plan-doc headers, Step-numbered handlers, and a fourth error idiom (phased-progress-frames and String errors) on top of the crate's existing three (Status, eyre, and the select-outcome Option<bool>). Headline findings: the deprecated Pull RPC carries ~500 lines of unreachable TCP data plane that is ALSO the only multi-stream pull implementation — the production pull_sync path runs a single stream ('multi-stream support lives in pull.rs'), a direct FAST violation born of an unfinished migration; the push path drags a vestigial 262,144-slot manifest channel whose receiver exists only to drain it; cross-crate, the daemon re-implements blit-app's manifest enumerator and delete-list applier (the latter already caused one containment-divergence CVE-class bug, R58-F3), hand-rolls the FilterSpec conversion that blit-core validates properly, and inherits the keepalive-less, decode-limit-less client channel for its own outbound delegated connects while its server side carefully configures keepalive. Searches run: full reads of all 17 service-layer and support files (admin.rs/runtime.rs/active_jobs.rs/delegation_gate.rs skimmed at heads plus targeted ranges); rg by API (max_decoding_message_size, keepalive, tcp_nodelay, read_exact token), by symbol (.pull(, scan_remote_files, enumerate_local_manifest, metadata_mtime_seconds, permissions_mode, filter conversions via min_age_secs), by constant (32*1024*1024*1024 ladder), by annotation (allow(dead_code), cfg(test)), and by string ([data-plane], eprintln counts, 'deprecated').
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:1151:  - evidence: pull.rs:694-696 calls its own RPC 'this deprecated-but-exposed Pull RPC path'. Repo-wide rg for `.pull(` shows the only Pull-RPC clients are blit-core/src/remote/pull.rs:305 (RemotePullClient::pull — itself uncalled by blit-app/blit-cli/blit-tui/tests; rg for `RemotePullClient::pull`, `.pull(`, `pull_with_options` across those crates returns nothing) and :491/:539 (scan_remote_files/open_remote_file, both hardcoding force_grpc=true so they take only the gRPC branch at pull.rs:85-105). The TCP-negotiation branches are unreachable from any in-repo caller. pull_sync.rs:567-568 confirms the half-migration: 'Single stream for the resume path (multi-stream support lives in pull.rs)' — the PRODUCTION pull path runs one TCP stream while multi-stream support survives only in the dead RPC.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:1152:  - status: Abandoned predecessor of PullSync kept for wire compatibility; its multi-stream data plane was never ported to pull_sync, so the live path is slower than the dead one — a FAST principle casualty of an unfinished migration.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:1180:- **Review-loop annotated era: comments citing finding IDs (R46-F1, R49-F3, R51-F4, R53-F1, R54-F1/F2, R58-F6/F7, audit-h1, audit-l39) and multi-paragraph remediation prose embedded in bail! messages; the run_X / run_X_deferred / print_deferred_X output-ordering pattern** — The transfer command path (copy/mirror/move) was hardened through many numbered review rounds by an author culture that records the finding ID at every gate. Error messages here are essays with per-direction remediation tables — strong RELIABLE alignment, but a completely different voice from the terse admin verbs. The deferred-output pattern (suppress the success summary until after source-delete) is replicated three times, once per transfer direction module.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:1185:- **A.0 extraction shims: `pub type X = <blit_app type>` aliases, one-function wrapper modules, re-export shims "so existing call sites stay working", and tombstone comments pointing at tests/logic that moved to blit-app** — A later era migrated CLI orchestration bodies into blit-app (the 'A.0 sub-slice') but left compatibility residue in place rather than updating call sites. The migration is also asymmetric: pull and delegated-pull outcome types are aliases to blit-app types, but the push outcome struct (DeferredPushState) is still defined locally, and blit-app's own module doc admits parts "stay in blit-cli until the M-C AppProgressEvent reshape". The ~160-line hand-rolled progress-monitor task is the largest piece still stranded on the CLI side of that unfinished boundary.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:1204:  - doc-comment style split: newer test files open with //! module docs naming the bug (single_file_copy.rs:1-5, local_move_semantics.rs:1-8, cli_arg_safety_gates.rs:1-8, diagnostics_dump.rs:1-5); the harness-clone files and blit_utils.rs have no module doc at all
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:1210:- byte/throughput formatter family: jobs.rs rolls a decimal-unit format_bps while the crate elsewhere imports blit-app's binary-unit format_bytes; blit-tui additionally carries three more private format_bytes copies. Net effect: one binary prints KiB in `blit ls` and KB in `blit jobs watch` — a SIMPLE/consistency violation — here: crates/blit-cli/src/jobs.rs:471-481 (format_bps, decimal); crates/blit-cli/src/ls.rs:3 + df.rs:3 + transfers/local.rs:3 (import blit_app::display::format_bytes, binary); elsewhere: crates/blit-app/src/display.rs:14-26 (canonical format_bytes); crates/blit-tui/src/screens/f2.rs:562, f3.rs:586, f4.rs:699 (three more private copies)
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:1222:  - evidence: crates/blit-cli/src/cli.rs:133-138 declares the flag and its help text says 'until then this flag controls the GetState polling cadence'; crates/blit-cli/src/jobs.rs:171-173 admits 'preserved on the CLI for backward compatibility but has no effect under the streaming model'; `rg interval_ms crates/blit-cli/src` shows the only other occurrence is that comment — no read anywhere
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:1228:  - evidence: crates/blit-cli/src/transfers/local.rs:186-187 sets both fields; crates/blit-core/src/orchestrator/options.rs:60-66 carries both with the R58-F7 comment; crates/blit-core/src/orchestrator/orchestrator.rs:516 calls the bool 'Backward-compat'
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:1241:  - status: Intentional dead code (defensive). Harmless, but the bail text ('listing a bare host requires list-modules or module/path syntax') can never be shown to a user, so it is untestable explanatory text — minor RELIABLE smell.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:1245:blit-app is the Phase-5 'A.0' extraction layer: 27 files, all but two of which are mechanical lifts out of blit-cli with heavy provenance doc-comments. Searches run: full read of every file in crates/blit-app/src; rg across all crates for format_bytes, connect_with_timeout/BlitClient::connect/Endpoint::from_shared/connect_timeout, keepalive/max_decoding, metadata_mtime_seconds, relative_path_to_posix, enumerate_local_manifest, FileHeader construction, WalkDir, remote_remote_direct, CONNECT_TIMEOUT, is_retryable, perf::query/PerfReport, clear_recent; per-symbol consumer checks of all major blit-app pub items in blit-cli and blit-tui; comparison reads of blit-core pull/push connect and blit-daemon delegated_pull. Strata found: (1) the A.0 extraction era (R-series finding IDs, narrative reviewer-round comments), (2) an 'audit-N' hardening era (client.rs, retry.rs — including an eprintln! that violates the crate's own no-stdout contract), (3) a lettered micro-slice jobs era (b-5/c-5/m-jobs-3, typed status-code mapping), plus two intra-crate idiom seams: bare context-free `eyre!(status.message())` errors across all browse verbs vs rich code-matched errors elsewhere (anti-RELIABLE), and two enumeration + three path-stringify + three mtime idioms coexisting (transfers/remote.rs ignores the canonical blit_core::path_posix helper its sibling endpoints.rs delegates to). Cross-crate: the crate hosts the 3rd of the repo's 3 channel builders (a 4th bounding idiom lives in blit-daemon net_timeout) — its connect_with_timeout is the most evolved but, like all clients, sets no HTTP/2 keepalive (daemon server does) and no max_decoding_message_size; enumerate_local_manifest is duplicated in blit-daemon with worse (sequential) performance; format_bytes is re-implemented four times in blit-tui despite blit-tui depending on blit-app's display module built for exactly that purpose; metadata_mtime_seconds exists in three crates. Dead/abandoned: the empty transfers/remote_remote_direct.rs stub (move from blit-cli never executed — the CLI copy is still live), diagnostics::perf::query/PerfReport with zero consumers, a stale #[allow(dead_code)] on the actively-used WatchSnapshot, an unused pub CONNECT_TIMEOUT that the three hardcoded 30s copies elsewhere should import, and several provenance comments pointing at deleted files (blit-cli/src/util.rs) or contradicting current code (check.rs one_way test comment).
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:1249:- **Phase-5 'A.0 extraction' narrative doc-comments: every module opens with 'Moved from crates/blit-cli/src/... in A.0', cites docs/plan/TUI_DESIGN.md §7, names the future TUI pane (F1-F4) that will consume it, and carries review-finding IDs of the R-series loop (R5-F1, R15-F1, R46-F3, R46-F6, R49, R53-F2, R58-F12, R59). Tests 'travel with the function' and carry their own provenance comments.** — The dominant layer: ~all of the crate is a mechanical lift out of blit-cli performed slice-by-slice by a review-loop agent. Style: eyre + with_context(|| format!(...)), spawn_blocking wrappers around sync core calls, 'primitive fields only — no clap, no presentation' execution structs (PullSyncExecution, PushExecution, DelegatedPullExecution), pub-widening notes ('was pub(crate)'). Doc comments narrate reviewer round-trips ('Round 1 ... round-2 split fixes that regression').
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:1253:  - crates/blit-app/src/transfers/remote.rs:914-920 test module: 'Moved from crates/blit-cli/src/transfers/remote.rs::delete_list_safety_tests in the a0-remote-helpers reopen pass'
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:1262:- **Two incompatible error-handling registers for the same operation class: bare `map_err(|status| eyre::eyre!(status.message().to_string()))` with zero context vs rich code-matched errors with endpoint context and recovery hints.** — Seam inside the admin layer. The five browse verbs (df, du, find, ls remote, list_modules) and jobs::query/subscribe all strip the gRPC status code and attach no 'while talking to <host>' context — a daemon-side InvalidArgument surfaces as a naked message with no indication of what failed (anti-RELIABLE). Meanwhile jobs::cancel, clear_recent, and everything in transfers/remote.rs match on Code:: and explain what to do. The two registers coexist file-by-file, suggesting the browse verbs were ported by one author and never revisited by the one who wrote the delegated-pull error mapper.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:1273:- enumerate_local_manifest — destination-tree walk producing Vec<FileHeader> for the pull-sync handshake. The daemon copy is an admitted 'mirror' but behaviorally divergent: blit-app's is rayon-parallel for checksums, the daemon's is sequential ('Parallelize later'), so identical workloads have different hardware efficiency depending on which side runs them (anti-FAST). — here: crates/blit-app/src/transfers/remote.rs:80-170; elsewhere: crates/blit-daemon/src/service/delegated_pull.rs:531-594 (doc comment at 531-536 names blit_app::transfers::remote::enumerate_local_manifest as the original)
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:1342:blit-prometheus-bridge is the cleanest, youngest stratum in the repo: 873 lines across three files, written entirely on 2026-05-23 by one review-loop sequence (bridge-1/2/3 feature slices plus an audit-5/6 hardening overlay, each layer tagged with finding-ID comments). It is internally consistent — eyre+Context errors, generic-over-future timeout helpers for testability, why-first comments, pub(crate) discipline — and, unlike the three independently-written gRPC channel builders elsewhere, it reuses blit-app's shared connect_with_timeout via jobs::query rather than rolling a fourth. The cost of that reuse: it inherits blit-app's channel gaps (no client-side HTTP/2 keepalive to match the daemon's server-side config at blit-daemon/src/main.rs:138, and tonic's default 4 MiB decode limit on GetState responses whose size scales with --recent-limit and unbounded error_message strings). The interesting seams it exposes are (1) the wire-level duplication of active-transfer count (Counters.active_transfers vs the ActiveTransfer list — proto/blit.proto:818 vs :747), which forces the bridge to distrust and omit all operation counters as documented false-zero risk, with the needed metrics_enabled signal already written daemon-side but parked under #[allow(dead_code)] (blit-daemon/src/metrics.rs:49); (2) four hand-rolled copies of the same timeout-to-eyre wrapper across this crate and blit-app; and (3) a stale cross-crate comment (server.rs:42 'jobs::query has no internal timeout') contradicted by blit-app's 30s CONNECT_TIMEOUT, plus the layered 8s-outside-30s deadline nesting where the inner bound can never fire. No dead code in the crate; no Prometheus-formatting duplication exists anywhere else in the workspace (verified by search). Against the project principles it scores well: SIMPLE (two flags, no tuning), RELIABLE (every wait bounded, fails to blit_daemon_up 0 or a hard one-shot error with clear text), FAST is moot at this scale.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:1375:  - status: Intentionally deferred, blocked on a proto change (add metrics_enabled to DaemonState or make Counters optional-when-disabled). The daemon-side is_enabled() accessor is the orphaned half: written, marked allow(dead_code), waiting for the wire field that was never added. Likely to stay frozen until someone files a proto slice.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:1441:- **[partial]** PushSummary carries transport stats (bytes/sec, zero-copy usage) for diagnostics
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:1442:  - evidence: proto/blit.proto:229-234 has bytes_zero_copy and tcp_fallback_used, but no bytes/sec rate field
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:1495:- **[unknown]** FAST principle: 'planner keeps perceived latency ≤ 1 s'
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:1617:- **[complete]** No integration tests cover remote transfer parity yet (open gap as of 2025-11-15)
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:1780:- **[abandoned]** Next step: open phase6/tui-rework branch and ship M1 first
docs/audit/DESIGN_FINDINGS_2026-06-11_PHASE_B.md:45:**Principle**: RELIABLE | **Slice**: medium
docs/audit/DESIGN_FINDINGS_2026-06-11_PHASE_B.md:61:**Principle**: FAST | **Slice**: medium
docs/audit/DESIGN_FINDINGS_2026-06-11_PHASE_B.md:78:**Principle**: FAST | **Slice**: large
docs/audit/DESIGN_FINDINGS_2026-06-11_PHASE_B.md:82:**Mechanism**: rg for `.pull(` workspace-wide finds only three gRPC-stub calls inside blit-core/src/remote/pull.rs itself: line 305 (inside the deprecated `pull` method at :251), line 491 (scan_remote_files) and line 539 (open_remote_file). The latter two hardcode force_grpc:true (:485, :530), so the daemon's Pull handler always takes the gRPC/non-streaming branches (daemon pull.rs:64 single-file, :85 force_grpc||metadata_only) and never reaches stream_pull_streaming (:208) or the TCP accepts (accept_pull_data_connection :625, accept_pull_data_connection_streaming :841, enumerate_to_channel :764, pull_stream_count :915). The only code that could send force_grpc=false is the deprecated client method, which nothing calls — pull.rs's own test doc at :1855 calls it 'the deprecated `pull` method', and the daemon comment at pull.rs:694-696 calls its server half 'this deprecated-but-exposed Pull RPC path'. Meanwhile the live PullSync handler negotiates stream_count=1 at both of its negotiation sites (pull_sync.rs:567-568 with the comment 'multi-stream support lives in pull.rs', and :707), even though the blit-core client side can receive multiple streams (receive_data_plane_streams_owned, pull.rs:1600-1646). Net: production pull runs one TCP stream; the up-to-16-stream ladder lives only in dead code. Wire-compat caveat: proto/blit.proto:11 still declares `rpc Pull`, so out-of-repo older clients could reach the TCP branches — retiring the RPC is an owner decision.
docs/audit/DESIGN_FINDINGS_2026-06-11_PHASE_B.md:86:- /home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-core/src/remote/pull.rs:485 — scan_remote_files: force_grpc: true — 'Force gRPC to get headers in the control stream'; open_remote_file same at :530
docs/audit/DESIGN_FINDINGS_2026-06-11_PHASE_B.md:95:**Principle**: RELIABLE | **Slice**: medium
docs/audit/DESIGN_FINDINGS_2026-06-11_PHASE_B.md:114:**Principle**: RELIABLE | **Slice**: medium
docs/audit/DESIGN_FINDINGS_2026-06-11_PHASE_B.md:130:**Principle**: FAST | **Slice**: medium
docs/audit/DESIGN_FINDINGS_2026-06-11_PHASE_B.md:145:**Principle**: RELIABLE | **Slice**: small
docs/audit/DESIGN_FINDINGS_2026-06-11_PHASE_B.md:161:**Principle**: FAST | **Slice**: small
docs/audit/DESIGN_FINDINGS_2026-06-11_PHASE_B.md:165:**Mechanism**: The `root.is_file()` branch (pull.rs:397-439) calls build_file_header(..., compute_checksums) directly (pull.rs:430); with checksums enabled build_file_header opens the file with std::fs::File::open and loops sync `reader.read(&mut [0u8; 256*1024])` + hasher.update until EOF (pull.rs:513-534) — for a 100 GB file that is the better part of a minute of uninterrupted blocking I/O+CPU on a runtime worker, during which every other future on that worker (other handlers, tickers, event fan-out) is frozen. The directory branch immediately below wraps the identical work in tokio::task::spawn_blocking with rayon par_iter (pull.rs:448-499), so the fix pattern is already in the same function. Reachable from legacy pull (pull.rs:373) and from pull_sync Phase 3 (pull_sync.rs:111) whenever the client requests ComparisonMode::Checksum and the server has checksums enabled. The sync std::fs::metadata pair at pull.rs:418-419 rides along.
docs/audit/DESIGN_FINDINGS_2026-06-11_PHASE_B.md:177:**Principle**: RELIABLE | **Slice**: small
docs/audit/DESIGN_FINDINGS_2026-06-11_PHASE_B.md:181:**Mechanism**: MultiStreamSender stores `pipeline_handle: Option<JoinHandle<Result<SinkOutcome>>>` (mod.rs:104) for the task spawned at mod.rs:156 that runs execute_sink_pipeline_streaming over N TCP sinks; spawn_response_task (helpers.rs:243) is likewise a bare tokio::spawn. The push() event loop has many `?`/return exits while `data_plane_sender` is still Some — e.g. a control-stream error returns at mod.rs:707, and every send_payload/queue `?` — at which point MultiStreamSender is dropped, dropping the JoinHandle, which detaches the task: the pipeline keeps preparing and writing all already-queued payloads (tar shards up to MiBs each, prefetch-deep) to the still-open TCP sockets. The workspace's own fix for this exact class, AbortOnDrop (pull.rs:31, R32-F2), is pub(crate) and used throughout pull.rs (:315, :726-732, :765, :1640-1657) but nowhere in push. Consequence: after a retryable control-plane error, blit_app::transfers::retry::run_with_retries (retry.rs:55-77) launches a fresh attempt while the orphaned pipeline of the failed attempt may still be writing the same destination files via the daemon's (also detached, design-2) data-plane workers — two unsynchronized writers per file plus double progress accounting.
docs/audit/DESIGN_FINDINGS_2026-06-11_PHASE_B.md:194:**Principle**: RELIABLE | **Slice**: small
docs/audit/DESIGN_FINDINGS_2026-06-11_PHASE_B.md:198:**Mechanism**: control.rs:55 creates `mpsc::channel::<FileHeader>(FILE_UPLOAD_CHANNEL_CAPACITY)` (= 16*1024*16 = 262,144, :31); every manifest entry passing file_requires_upload is sent at :157 *before* the transfer-mode branch. In fallback mode (client --force-grpc, daemon force_grpc_data, or the automatic bind-failure fallback at :181-199) `upload_rx_opt.take()` is never executed (:214/:287 are TCP-only), so the receiver stays alive-but-unread in the local until the function returns: send #262,145 awaits forever. The daemon stops reading the request stream, gRPC flow control backpressures the client's manifest sends, and both sides wedge with no timeout in scope — HTTP/2 keepalive (main.rs:137-142) sees a healthy connection, StallGuard covers TCP data planes only. In TCP mode the consumer (data_plane.rs:89, :164) wraps the receiver in Arc<AsyncMutex>, each of N workers spawns a task (:200-206) whose only body is `while guard.recv().await.is_some() {}` (N-1 of them blocked on the mutex), and the companion `cache` is explicitly voided (:207) — the comment at control.rs:150-156 admits 'Only the gRPC fallback path uses this queue', which is false: the fallback path is the one that never reads it.
docs/audit/DESIGN_FINDINGS_2026-06-11_PHASE_B.md:210:**Principle**: RELIABLE | **Slice**: medium
docs/audit/DESIGN_FINDINGS_2026-06-11_PHASE_B.md:214:**Mechanism**: blit-daemon depends only on blit-core (Cargo.toml:10), so the delegated-pull path re-implements blit-app logic: delegated_pull.rs:531-537 admits its enumerate_local_manifest is a 'Mirror of blit_app::transfers::remote::enumerate_local_manifest' but sequential ('Parallelize later'), while the blit-app twin (remote.rs:80-99) computes checksums rayon-parallel — identical workloads get different hardware efficiency depending on which side of a delegation runs them (FAST). Worse precedent: apply_delete_list's own doc (delegated_pull.rs:431-445) records that the blit-app twin upgraded to safe_join_contained in R46-F3 while the daemon copy stayed on bare safe_join, leaving a symlink-escape deletion hole until R58-F3 re-synced them — direct in-repo proof this duplication produces security drift, and nothing structural prevents the next fix landing on one side only. The destructive-path logic belongs in the crate both binaries already share.
docs/audit/DESIGN_FINDINGS_2026-06-11_PHASE_B.md:226:**Principle**: SIMPLE | **Slice**: small
docs/audit/DESIGN_FINDINGS_2026-06-11_PHASE_B.md:241:**Principle**: RELIABLE | **Slice**: medium
docs/audit/DESIGN_FINDINGS_2026-06-11_PHASE_B.md:243:**Claim**: blit-core's ProgressEvent enum carries no semantic contract, and its producers assign three incompatible meanings to the same variants, so each consumer must hard-code per-direction knowledge of blit-core internals — the structural cause of the already-filed CLI double-count.
docs/audit/DESIGN_FINDINGS_2026-06-11_PHASE_B.md:256:**Principle**: FAST | **Slice**: medium
docs/audit/DESIGN_FINDINGS_2026-06-11_PHASE_B.md:273:**Principle**: RELIABLE | **Slice**: small
docs/audit/DESIGN_FINDINGS_2026-06-11_PHASE_B.md:304:**Principle**: SIMPLE | **Slice**: small
docs/audit/DESIGN_FINDINGS_2026-06-11_PHASE_B.md:336:**Principle**: SIMPLE | **Slice**: small
docs/audit/DESIGN_FINDINGS_2026-06-11_PHASE_B.md:352:**Principle**: SIMPLE | **Slice**: medium
docs/audit/DESIGN_FINDINGS_2026-06-11_PHASE_B.md:366:#### deadcode-core-abandoned-foundation-modules — Four whole blit-core modules plus copy/fs_enum leftovers (~1,000 lines) have zero callers: tar_stream, zero_copy, delete, copy/parallel+stats, chunked_copy_file, fs_enum helpers
docs/audit/DESIGN_FINDINGS_2026-06-11_PHASE_B.md:370:**Claim**: tar_stream.rs (414 lines), zero_copy.rs (219), delete.rs (93), copy/parallel.rs+stats.rs (68), chunked_copy_file, and four fs_enum helpers are all pub-exported with zero callers outside their own files/re-exports, each superseded by a live implementation elsewhere.
docs/audit/DESIGN_FINDINGS_2026-06-11_PHASE_B.md:372:**Mechanism**: Per-symbol rg across crates/ and tests/ this session: (1) tar_stream/TarConfig/TarEvent — only lib.rs:23; its header cites a parent file streaming_batch.rs that no longer exists; live tar shards go through remote/transfer/payload.rs + tar_safety. (2) zero_copy::/splice_from_socket_to_file/ZeroCopyResult/AsRawFileDescriptor — only lib.rs:27; the receive pipeline never adopted splice. (3) crate::delete/DeletePlan/compute_delete_plan/generate_delete_plan — only lib.rs:7; mirror deletion actually flows through MirrorPlanner (orchestrator/fast_path.rs:104). (4) parallel_copy_files/CopyStats/chunked_copy_file — only the definitions and copy/mod.rs:11-14 re-exports; the live local fast path is copy_paths_blocking/copy_file (orchestrator.rs:355, :1263) and the live parallel path is execute_sink_pipeline (transfer/pipeline.rs:24). (5) categorize_files (fs_enum.rs:498), enumerate_symlinks+SymlinkEntry (:20, :439, :475), enumerate_directory_deref_filtered (:521) — zero hits outside fs_enum.rs; the size split lives in transfer_plan.rs now. All compile-checked dead only because they are pub. zero_copy is the one needing an owner decision: delete, or revive as a FAST-principle feature.
docs/audit/DESIGN_FINDINGS_2026-06-11_PHASE_B.md:379:- /home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-core/src/zero_copy.rs:1 — 'Zero-copy primitives for high-performance I/O' — 219 lines, only lib.rs:27 references it; delete-vs-revive is an owner decision (FAST)
docs/audit/DESIGN_FINDINGS_2026-06-11_PHASE_B.md:381:**Proposed fix**: One deletion slice removing tar_stream.rs, delete.rs, copy/parallel.rs, copy/stats.rs, chunked_copy_file, and the four fs_enum helpers plus their lib.rs/copy/mod.rs exports (pure dead weight, no wire impact); a separate owner question for zero_copy.rs: delete now or file a plan to wire splice into the receive pipeline.

docs/audit/DESIGN_FINDINGS_2026-06-11_PHASE_B.md:514:**Principle**: FAST | **Slice**: medium
docs/audit/DESIGN_FINDINGS_2026-06-11_PHASE_B.md:551:**Mechanism**: checksum.rs declares `vec![0u8; 256 * 1024]` and the identical read/update loop at lines 148 (Blake3), 160 (XxHash3), 174 (Md5), and 194 (partial hash). blit-daemon/src/service/pull.rs:521-534 re-implements Blake3 whole-file hashing from scratch — blake3::Hasher::new + BufReader + `let mut buf = [0u8; 256 * 1024]` stack array — instead of calling checksum::hash_file (it wants the already-open File, which hash_file's path-based signature cannot accept). Five sites re-state the 256 KiB buffer decision; a change in checksum strategy (e.g. mmap, rayon-chunked Blake3) would not reach the daemon's pull checksum path, silently desynchronizing client/daemon checksums' performance characteristics, and the daemon copy puts a 256 KiB frame on the stack of an async-adjacent task.
docs/audit/DESIGN_FINDINGS_2026-06-11_PHASE_B.md:557:**Proposed fix**: Add a hash_reader(reader: impl Read, ty: ChecksumType) helper in checksum.rs that owns the buffer size and loop; hash_file and the daemon's build_file_header both call it (daemon passes its already-open File).
docs/audit/DESIGN_FINDINGS_2026-06-11_PHASE_B.md:561:**Principle**: RELIABLE | **Slice**: medium
docs/audit/DESIGN_FINDINGS_2026-06-11_PHASE_B.md:577:**Principle**: RELIABLE | **Slice**: medium
docs/audit/DESIGN_FINDINGS_2026-06-11_PHASE_B.md:581:**Mechanism**: progress_accum.rs:21-36 (pull: bytes from Payload only — its doc at lines 12-20 explicitly names the pipeline.rs double-emit trap), :50-65 (push: bytes+files from FileComplete only), :75-88 (delegated: both from Payload) encode three incompatible producer semantics. blit-cli/transfers/remote.rs:64-76 uses a single rule for all directions that adds bytes from BOTH Payload and FileComplete — the exact combination progress_accum.rs:12-16 documents as double-counting on the TCP pull receive path (the resulting CLI bug is filed as design-1-cli-pull-byte-double-count; this finding is about the structure that produced it). The semantics are documented nowhere on ProgressEvent itself (blit-core/src/remote/transfer/progress.rs), only in TUI-crate doc comments, so fixing design-1 inside the CLI would create a third independent copy of the folding rules rather than a shared owner.
docs/audit/DESIGN_FINDINGS_2026-06-11_PHASE_B.md:607:**Principle**: RELIABLE | **Slice**: medium

docs/audit/DESIGN_FINDINGS_2026-06-11_PHASE_B.md:709:**Mechanism**: rg for 'jobs|Subscribe|cancel' across blit-cli/tests hits only remote_remote.rs, and those hits (e.g. :649, :717) are the fake server's unimplemented trait-method stubs, not jobs-verb tests. No test passes 'jobs' or '--detach' to the CLI binary (rg 'detach' over tests: zero hits). What exists: blit-cli/src/jobs.rs:795+ unit tests (pure formatting/exit-code mapping) and blit-daemon/src/active_jobs.rs unit tests (29, in-process registry). The wire path — jobs watch opening Subscribe, the stream-error fallback to one final GetState (jobs.rs:348-362), cancel_exit_code's 0/1 contract against a real daemon's CancelJob — runs in zero tests. Given the already-filed design-2 (orphaned daemon data planes, cancellation reaching one of four transfer kinds), the team is actively changing cancellation behavior with no harness to detect regressions in the user-facing verbs.

docs/audit/DESIGN_FINDINGS_2026-06-11_PHASE_B.md:879:**Mechanism**: cli.rs:133-138 declares `#[arg(long, default_value_t = 1000)] pub interval_ms: u64` with doc-comment help text: 'A future milestone-C Subscribe RPC will replace polling ... until then this flag controls the GetState polling cadence.' That future already arrived: jobs.rs:171-173 states 'args.interval_ms is preserved on the CLI for backward compatibility but has no effect under the streaming model — Subscribe pushes; no polling cadence to configure.' rg for interval_ms across crates/blit-cli/src confirms those are the only two occurrences — no code reads the value. So clap accepts and silently ignores it while --help promises an effect: a plain-failure/honest-text violation. Supersessor: the Subscribe stream (jobs.rs watch loop) with --timeout-secs as the only live knob.
docs/audit/DESIGN_FINDINGS_2026-06-11_PHASE_B.md:883:- /home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-cli/src/jobs.rs:171 — 'preserved on the CLI for backward compatibility but has no effect under the streaming model' — the only other interval_ms occurrence in the crate
docs/audit/DESIGN_FINDINGS_2026-06-11_PHASE_B.md:885:**Proposed fix**: Either remove the flag (owner call on CLI backward compatibility) or keep it hidden/deprecated with corrected help text; per the repo's docs rule, the same slice must update --help, the manpage, and README together.

docs/audit/DESIGN_FINDINGS_2026-06-11_PHASE_B.md:918:**Principle**: SIMPLE | **Slice**: small
docs/audit/DESIGN_FINDINGS_2026-06-11_PHASE_B.md:922:**Mechanism**: rg for analyze_warmup_result hits only its definition (auto_tune/mod.rs:26) plus nothing else. rg for determine_tuning shows the sole production caller is remote/tuning.rs:13, which passes None — so warmup_gbps is always None, making the bandwidth-keyed initial_streams (mod.rs:45-55) and tcp_buffer_size/prefetch ladders (mod.rs:57-67) unreachable outside tests (mod.rs:85 passes Some in a test). determine_remote_tuning then overwrites initial_streams/max_streams from its own byte ladder (tuning.rs:27-28) and tcp_buffer_size/prefetch_count (tuning.rs:30-36), so even the None-branch outputs (initial_streams=2, max_streams=8, buffers None) never survive. All remote sizing is the static byte-keyed table in tuning.rs; the module doc (mod.rs:3 'Provides warmup probes') advertises adaptivity the system does not have — a SIMPLE-principle lie in the code. Cross-ref: the queued slice-2 'adaptive windows' transport work is about TCP/HTTP2 windows on channels, not this warmup path; no overlap.
