The bounded-unilateral dial contract (D-2026-06-20-1/-2) is NOT superseded — it carries into the unified session unchanged.
.review/results/d-2026-07-05-2-compat.codex.md:497:docs/DECISIONS.md:134:## D-2026-07-05-2 — No version compatibility, ever: same-build peers only
.review/results/d-2026-07-05-2-compat.codex.md:498:docs/DECISIONS.md:135:- Decision: Blit has NO version-compatibility obligation of any kind, in any direction, at any time — owner standing rule, restated with force 2026-07-05: "backward compatibility is NOT a consideration. I expect blit 1.2.3 not to be able to talk to blit-daemon 1.2.3.1. period. same build only. do not engineer tech debt into an unshipped product." Client and daemon interoperate only when built from the same source; the wire handshake must REFUSE a mismatched peer outright at session open (exact protocol/build identity — mechanism specified in ONE_TRANSFER_PATH otp-1 and pinned by test). Feature-capability bits that exist to tolerate version skew ("advisory until both peers advertise support", `supports_stream_resize`-style flags) are dead weight and go away with the unified session. NOT affected: the receiver capacity profile (runtime capacity of the receiving machine, D-2026-06-20-1/-2) — that is hardware negotiation, not version negotiation.
.review/results/d-2026-07-05-2-compat.codex.md:499:docs/DECISIONS.md:136:- Why: REV4 §Constraints carried a written "mixed old/new peers must negotiate down" rule while the owner's contrary rule lived only in chat; the ONE_TRANSFER_PATH plan review then resolved the document conflict in favor of the written rule ("governs until cutover"). Wrong direction — recording the owner's rule as a decision ends the unrecorded-intent-loses-to-stale-paper failure mode.
.review/results/d-2026-07-05-2-compat.codex.md:500:docs/DECISIONS.md:137:- Supersedes: REV4 §Constraints mixed-version clause (annotated in place, effective immediately — not at cutover); SMALL_FILE_CEILING §Constraints "mixed-version peers keep working via existing negotiation" clause and sf-6's mixed-version-test deliverable (annotated); the "effective only at ONE_TRANSFER_PATH's cutover slice" scoping inside D-2026-07-05-1's Supersedes line (the supersession is immediate and total); ONE_TRANSFER_PATH's Non-goals compat wording (rewritten same commit).
.review/results/d-2026-07-05-2-compat.codex.md:516:docs/reviews/followup_review_2026-05-02.md:366:the wire boundary. With no backward-compatibility requirement, this should be

.review/results/d-2026-07-05-2-compat.codex.md:533:docs/reviews/followup_review_2026-05-02.md:1620:### R21-F5. Automatic fallback on Unimplemented preserves old-daemon compatibility despite the release premise
.review/results/d-2026-07-05-2-compat.codex.md:534:docs/reviews/followup_review_2026-05-02.md:1630:debt for backwards compatibility. Keeping `--relay-via-cli` for topology

.review/results/d-2026-07-05-2-compat.codex.md:543:docs/reviews/followup_review_2026-05-02.md:1712:gate does not solve), 4.3.4 (docs requirement), 4.3.5 (forward-compat
.review/results/d-2026-07-05-2-compat.codex.md:547:docs/reviews/followup_review_2026-05-02.md:1780:(proto3 silently preserves them; that's not a compatibility strategy)."
.review/results/d-2026-07-05-2-compat.codex.md:569:docs/audit/AUDIT_REPORT_2026-06-11_DESIGN.md:70:- **W2.4** (H, medium — owner decision on wire compat, proto not frozen per
.review/results/d-2026-07-05-2-compat.codex.md:581:docs/audit/findings/inconsistency-timeouts.md:150:### dead-watch-flag-interval-ms — `jobs watch --interval-ms` is a dead flag preserved for back-compat
.review/results/d-2026-07-05-2-compat.codex.md:582:docs/audit/findings/inconsistency-timeouts.md:154:  1. `crates/blit-cli/src/cli.rs:118-124` — `--interval-ms` defaults to 1000, doc-comment says "preserved for backward compat per comment but does nothing under the streaming Subscribe model". `code-cli.md` inventory flagged this as a smell.
.review/results/d-2026-07-05-2-compat.codex.md:583:docs/audit/findings/inconsistency-timeouts.md:157:**Canonical**: Either remove the flag (back-compat break) or hide it (`hide = true` in clap derive) so users don't see it in help and tune-tune-tune with no effect.
.review/results/d-2026-07-05-2-compat.codex.md:587:docs/audit/DESIGN_FINDINGS_2026-06-11_PHASE_B.md:82:**Mechanism**: rg for `.pull(` workspace-wide finds only three gRPC-stub calls inside blit-core/src/remote/pull.rs itself: line 305 (inside the deprecated `pull` method at :251), line 491 (scan_remote_files) and line 539 (open_remote_file). The latter two hardcode force_grpc:true (:485, :530), so the daemon's Pull handler always takes the gRPC/non-streaming branches (daemon pull.rs:64 single-file, :85 force_grpc||metadata_only) and never reaches stream_pull_streaming (:208) or the TCP accepts (accept_pull_data_connection :625, accept_pull_data_connection_streaming :841, enumerate_to_channel :764, pull_stream_count :915). The only code that could send force_grpc=false is the deprecated client method, which nothing calls — pull.rs's own test doc at :1855 calls it 'the deprecated `pull` method', and the daemon comment at pull.rs:694-696 calls its server half 'this deprecated-but-exposed Pull RPC path'. Meanwhile the live PullSync handler negotiates stream_count=1 at both of its negotiation sites (pull_sync.rs:567-568 with the comment 'multi-stream support lives in pull.rs', and :707), even though the blit-core client side can receive multiple streams (receive_data_plane_streams_owned, pull.rs:1600-1646). Net: production pull runs one TCP stream; the up-to-16-stream ladder lives only in dead code. Wire-compat caveat: proto/blit.proto:11 still declares `rpc Pull`, so out-of-repo older clients could reach the TCP branches — retiring the RPC is an owner decision.
.review/results/d-2026-07-05-2-compat.codex.md:588:docs/audit/DESIGN_FINDINGS_2026-06-11_PHASE_B.md:214:**Mechanism**: blit-daemon depends only on blit-core (Cargo.toml:10), so the delegated-pull path re-implements blit-app logic: delegated_pull.rs:531-537 admits its enumerate_local_manifest is a 'Mirror of blit_app::transfers::remote::enumerate_local_manifest' but sequential ('Parallelize later'), while the blit-app twin (remote.rs:80-99) computes checksums rayon-parallel — identical workloads get different hardware efficiency depending on which side of a delegation runs them (FAST). Worse precedent: apply_delete_list's own doc (delegated_pull.rs:431-445) records that the blit-app twin upgraded to safe_join_contained in R46-F3 while the daemon copy stayed on bare safe_join, leaving a symlink-escape deletion hole until R58-F3 re-synced them — direct in-repo proof this duplication produces security drift, and nothing structural prevents the next fix landing on one side only. The destructive-path logic belongs in the crate both binaries already share.
.review/results/d-2026-07-05-2-compat.codex.md:590:docs/audit/DESIGN_FINDINGS_2026-06-11_PHASE_B.md:243:**Claim**: blit-core's ProgressEvent enum carries no semantic contract, and its producers assign three incompatible meanings to the same variants, so each consumer must hard-code per-direction knowledge of blit-core internals — the structural cause of the already-filed CLI double-count.
.review/results/d-2026-07-05-2-compat.codex.md:601:docs/audit/DESIGN_FINDINGS_2026-06-11_PHASE_B.md:581:**Mechanism**: progress_accum.rs:21-36 (pull: bytes from Payload only — its doc at lines 12-20 explicitly names the pipeline.rs double-emit trap), :50-65 (push: bytes+files from FileComplete only), :75-88 (delegated: both from Payload) encode three incompatible producer semantics. blit-cli/transfers/remote.rs:64-76 uses a single rule for all directions that adds bytes from BOTH Payload and FileComplete — the exact combination progress_accum.rs:12-16 documents as double-counting on the TCP pull receive path (the resulting CLI bug is filed as design-1-cli-pull-byte-double-count; this finding is about the structure that produced it). The semantics are documented nowhere on ProgressEvent itself (blit-core/src/remote/transfer/progress.rs), only in TUI-crate doc comments, so fixing design-1 inside the CLI would create a third independent copy of the folding rules rather than a shared owner.
.review/results/d-2026-07-05-2-compat.codex.md:603:docs/audit/DESIGN_FINDINGS_2026-06-11_PHASE_B.md:879:**Mechanism**: cli.rs:133-138 declares `#[arg(long, default_value_t = 1000)] pub interval_ms: u64` with doc-comment help text: 'A future milestone-C Subscribe RPC will replace polling ... until then this flag controls the GetState polling cadence.' That future already arrived: jobs.rs:171-173 states 'args.interval_ms is preserved on the CLI for backward compatibility but has no effect under the streaming model — Subscribe pushes; no polling cadence to configure.' rg for interval_ms across crates/blit-cli/src confirms those are the only two occurrences — no code reads the value. So clap accepts and silently ignores it while --help promises an effect: a plain-failure/honest-text violation. Supersessor: the Subscribe stream (jobs.rs watch loop) with --timeout-secs as the only live knob.
.review/results/d-2026-07-05-2-compat.codex.md:604:docs/audit/DESIGN_FINDINGS_2026-06-11_PHASE_B.md:883:- /home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-cli/src/jobs.rs:171 — 'preserved on the CLI for backward compatibility but has no effect under the streaming model' — the only other interval_ms occurrence in the crate
.review/results/d-2026-07-05-2-compat.codex.md:605:docs/audit/DESIGN_FINDINGS_2026-06-11_PHASE_B.md:885:**Proposed fix**: Either remove the flag (owner call on CLI backward compatibility) or keep it hidden/deprecated with corrected help text; per the repo's docs rule, the same slice must update --help, the manpage, and README together.
.review/results/d-2026-07-05-2-compat.codex.md:607:docs/audit/DESIGN_FINDINGS_2026-06-11_PHASE_B.md:922:**Mechanism**: rg for analyze_warmup_result hits only its definition (auto_tune/mod.rs:26) plus nothing else. rg for determine_tuning shows the sole production caller is remote/tuning.rs:13, which passes None — so warmup_gbps is always None, making the bandwidth-keyed initial_streams (mod.rs:45-55) and tcp_buffer_size/prefetch ladders (mod.rs:57-67) unreachable outside tests (mod.rs:85 passes Some in a test). determine_remote_tuning then overwrites initial_streams/max_streams from its own byte ladder (tuning.rs:27-28) and tcp_buffer_size/prefetch_count (tuning.rs:30-36), so even the None-branch outputs (initial_streams=2, max_streams=8, buffers None) never survive. All remote sizing is the static byte-keyed table in tuning.rs; the module doc (mod.rs:3 'Provides warmup probes') advertises adaptivity the system does not have — a SIMPLE-principle lie in the code. Cross-ref: the queued slice-2 'adaptive windows' transport work is about TCP/HTTP2 windows on channels, not this warmup path; no overlap.
.review/results/d-2026-07-05-2-compat.codex.md:611:docs/audit/DESIGN_FINDINGS_2026-06-11_PHASE_B.md:1183:Scope: verified every entry in the design map's Part 2 Dead/abandoned lists for blit-core, blit-daemon, blit-cli, and blit-app, re-deriving each from code (rg caller searches + file reads) rather than trusting the map. VERIFIED-DEAD and reported: blit-core errors.rs, tar_stream.rs, zero_copy.rs, delete.rs, copy/parallel.rs+stats.rs, chunked_copy_file, fs_enum categorize_files/enumerate_symlinks/SymlinkEntry/enumerate_directory_deref_filtered, auto_tune warmup machinery, transfer_payloads_via_control_plane, RemotePullClient::pull + daemon legacy Pull TCP plane (with the pull_sync single-stream FAST consequence and the proto wire-compat owner decision), daemon push upload channel + drain task, daemon dead items behind allow(dead_code) (ModuleOptOut, resolve_contained_wire, acquire_buffer, ActiveJobs::cancel/as_str), CLI --interval-ms, CLI unused deps, blit-app empty remote_remote_direct stub + perf::query/PerfReport + stale WatchSnapshot allow. CHECKED AND FOUND LIVE (clean — valuable for Phase C): manifest.rs (consumed by daemon pull_sync); copy_file/copy_paths_blocking/resume_copy_file/mmap_copy_file (live local fast path via orchestrator.rs:355/:1263, local_worker.rs); scan_remote_files and open_remote_file (live, but force_grpc=true only); build_spec_from_options (live in blit-app, blit-tui, daemon); pull_sync client multi-stream receive machinery (pull.rs:1600-1646 — capable but never fed >1 stream by the daemon, folded into the Pull finding); WatchSnapshot, spawn_progress_ticker, active_jobs snapshot/recent/transfer_id/bytes_counter (live — only their allow annotations are stale); cancel_authorized (live at core.rs); push/data_plane.rs+push/payload.rs re-export shims (alive as indirection; judged low severity, dropped); blit-cli endpoints.rs wrapper module, DeferredPullState/DeferredDelegatedState aliases, rm.rs re-export (alive A.0 shims, low, dropped); tests/blit_utils.rs (runs and partially unique — overlap is a test-hygiene issue, judged low for this dimension); ls.rs defensive unreachable Discovery arm (intentional, low, dropped); buffer.rs BufferPool stats counters (vestigial but low, dropped). NOT RE-REPORTED per instructions: design-1/2/3 findings and all queued slice-2 transport items — notably I confirmed blit-app client::CONNECT_TIMEOUT has zero external consumers but folded that into the queued shared-channel-builder work instead of filing it. blit-tui: light pass only — confirmed it consumes RemotePullClient::build_spec_from_options and blit_app::profile (no TUI-internal dead-code findings filed per the Phase-6 rule). Not covered: blit-prometheus-bridge (map reports no dead list for it; did not independently sweep), Windows-only win_fs paths (cannot exercise; caller search only), and git-history dating of modules (read-only session, relied on map dates only for narrative, not for any claim).
.review/results/d-2026-07-05-2-compat.codex.md:612:docs/audit/DESIGN_FINDINGS_2026-06-11_PHASE_B.md:1187:Checked and found clean: (1) Local mirror deletion semantics — orchestrator.rs has a substantial cfg(test) suite (lines 1300+, 1971+) including mirror_still_deletes_truly_unrelated_destination_dirs (:1616), mirror_refuses_when_source_scan_incomplete (:1448), local_mirror_all_scope_deletes_through_filter (:1753), compare-mode and ignore-existing tests; diff_planner.rs has 14 unit tests covering plan_local_mirror; local_move_semantics.rs covers move-vs-mirror regression (R46-F1). (2) Force-gRPC paths ARE integration-tested (contrary to the dimension brief's suspicion): remote_parity.rs test_pull_grpc_fallback/:92 and test_push_grpc_fallback/:133, remote_tcp_fallback.rs --force-grpc-data/:137 and --force-grpc/:168, remote_resume.rs gRPC-fallback resume/:91 — but ALL unix-gated (folded into the cfg(unix) finding). (3) Remote pull-mirror purge semantics well covered on unix (remote_pull_mirror.rs:46,:342,:404 cover purge, FilteredSubset preservation, delete-scope-all). (4) Push mirror safety F1/F2 (incomplete-scan refusal, filtered enumeration) covered in remote_push_mirror_safety.rs. (5) Daemon unit-test density is good: active_jobs.rs 29, delegation_gate.rs 31, core.rs 25, pull_sync.rs 6, push/data_plane.rs 13. (6) Checksum negotiation ack flow covered (remote_checksum_negotiation.rs). Notable non-finding per instructions: multi-stream pull is NOT a test gap — the live PullSync path hard-codes stream_count=1 (pull_sync.rs:568, :707) and the multi-stream machinery lives only in the deprecated Pull client (blit-core pull.rs:251/1600) which has zero callers outside its own file (rg verified) — that is a Phase B dead-code question, already adjacent to the map's 'deprecated pull client path' dead-weight list. Map claim correction: 'all remote integration tests are cfg(unix)' is overstated — remote_move.rs, remote_pull_subpath.rs, admin_verbs.rs, and blit_utils.rs spawn daemons ungated and run on Windows CI; my cfg(unix) finding is re-derived around the files that are gated without unix APIs. Not covered: blit-tui test quality (light-pass rule; it has many in-crate tests, e.g. main.rs:8736+ purge-safety assertions, left to Phase 6), blit-prometheus-bridge tests, the .ps1 journal/USN scripts' coverage, and proto-level compatibility tests (no buf/breaking-change gate exists, but I could not size that without speculating). I did not run any cargo commands (read-only constraint), so test counts are from source inspection, not execution.
.review/results/d-2026-07-05-2-compat.codex.md:624:docs/audit/inventory/plan-wire.md:1226:Previous `PullSyncHeader` bool-soup shape was removed entirely because "we don't carry backward compatibility into the next release." Leading message is now TransferOperationSpec.
.review/results/d-2026-07-05-2-compat.codex.md:625:docs/audit/inventory/plan-wire.md:1250:`bytes delegated_credential` field (forward-compat auth-passthrough hook for the removed BlitAuth service) removed 2026-05-13. Reserved so it can't be reused for unrelated semantics.
.review/results/d-2026-07-05-2-compat.codex.md:629:docs/audit/AUDIT_REPORT_2026-06-04.md:5:Blit ships a coherent, well-tested 0.1.0 surface (hybrid TCP + gRPC transport, unified receive pipeline, always-on F2 containment, delegated remote→remote with explicit-only fallback, robust per-RPC daemon RPCs), but the plan corpus has not kept up with what shipped. The single largest gap is **TUI**: the rework's "Pick-not-Type" dual-pane shell is the default screen yet has no transfer-execution wiring (action bar is render-only), and the design's `detach=true` for remote→remote TUI delegations is hardcoded `false` — both undermine the "single pane of glass survives initiator disconnect" promise. Lower-altitude but pervasive drift: the planner's documented "1 s heartbeat / 10 s stall detector" architecture was never built (we ship a synchronous orchestrator with a 30 s data-plane idle guard on pull only), `--detach` shipped despite being "out of scope" in REMOTE_REMOTE_DELEGATION_PLAN, the manpage omits eight transfer flags including data-loss-class ones (`--null`, `--detach`, `--delete-scope`, `--force`, `--ignore-existing`) and two whole verbs (`jobs`, `check`), and the architecture/whitepaper still narrate auth/TLS planning that was explicitly removed from scope. Cross-cutting inconsistencies cluster around five seams — path rendering (3 ad-hoc `Path→wire` helpers with different empty-path encodings), error wrapping (admin verbs strip Status codes while jobs verbs preserve them, breaking `is_retryable`), endpoint parse Err handling (TUI F1 confirm-detail silently classifies as Local, violating an explicit project-memory rule reopened 4×), timeout/keepalive symmetry (stall guard only on pull-receive, TCP keepalive only on push-receive accept, three independent `connect_with_timeout` implementations), and destructive-action prompts (CLI `perf --clear` fires silently while TUI confirms; no `--yes` parity for `jobs clear-recent`/`jobs cancel`). What's solid: the data-plane wire format, F2 containment chokepoint, delegation gate with DNS-rebinding mitigation, spec-version fail-closed, `pull_sync_with_spec` endpoint-isolation, BlitAuth removal, and the CLI's documented data-loss reject-gates on `move` are all faithfully implemented and tested.
.review/results/d-2026-07-05-2-compat.codex.md:631:docs/audit/AUDIT_REPORT_2026-06-04.md:279:**Where**: `crates/blit-daemon/src/service/push/control.rs:343-345` (treats Unspecified|Off-with-mirror_mode=true as "use user's filter" — back-compat for older clients) vs `crates/blit-daemon/src/service/pull_sync.rs:430-462` (`Off | Unspecified` → `Vec::new()`)

.review/results/d-2026-07-05-2-compat.codex.md:713:docs/plan/ONE_TRANSFER_PATH.md:8:outright by **D-2026-07-05-2 (no version compatibility, ever — same

.review/results/d-2026-07-05-2-compat.codex.md:715:docs/plan/ONE_TRANSFER_PATH.md:49:  rule: "backward compatibility is NOT a consideration... same build

.review/results/d-2026-07-05-2-compat.codex.md:801:docs/audit/findings/drift-principles.md:155:- RELEASE_PLAN_v2_2026-05-04.md §2.8 line 436: "Silent dead code is incompatible with the 'no tech debt for the sake of backwards compatibility' release directive."
.review/results/d-2026-07-05-2-compat.codex.md:802:docs/audit/findings/drift-principles.md:167:**Notes**: Direct violation of `invariant-no-tech-debt-for-back-compat`. Either: (a) remove the `Pull` RPC and its message types entirely, OR (b) mark them `[deprecated = true]` so downstream tonic-prost generates `#[deprecated]` attributes that surface in user warnings. Currently it's the worst of both: live in proto, dead in policy.

.review/results/d-2026-07-05-2-compat.codex.md:829:docs/plan/DESIGN_COHERENCE_REVIEW.md:140:disposition, proto compatibility) were answered by the owner 2026-06-11 and
.review/results/d-2026-07-05-2-compat.codex.md:832:docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:148:- Push/pull socket asymmetry: TCP_NODELAY, SO_KEEPALIVE, and auto-tuned SO_SNDBUF/SO_RCVBUF are set on push-direction sockets only (client data_plane.rs:98-121, daemon push/data_plane.rs:118-120). The pull and pull-sync data sockets on BOTH ends (pull.rs:1710 client; daemon pull.rs accept_and_wrap_sinks, pull_sync.rs both paths) get kernel defaults — Nagle on, no keepalive, default windows. FAST violation: pull throughput is systematically untuned relative to push on identical hardware, and the auto-tuner's tcp_buffer_size output is silently discarded for pulls.
.review/results/d-2026-07-05-2-compat.codex.md:839:docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:327:- The advertised adaptivity is dead code: determine_tuning's warmup branches and analyze_warmup_result (auto_tune/mod.rs:26-67) have no production callers — remote/tuning.rs:13 always passes None, then overwrites the stream counts determine_tuning returned. The 'auto_tune' module name promises runtime adaptation (SIMPLE principle) the system does not perform; all sizing is a static size-keyed table.
.review/results/d-2026-07-05-2-compat.codex.md:840:docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:369:- `analyze_warmup_result thresholds` = >=6 Gbps: 32 MiB, else 16 MiB (/home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-core/src/auto_tune/mod.rs:26) — SIMPLE: dead code (zero callers) that advertises the adaptive behavior the project promises but does not have
.review/results/d-2026-07-05-2-compat.codex.md:842:docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:438:- --retry/--wait is silently inert on the primary gRPC paths. Every tonic::Status->String conversion (pull.rs:307/332/493/507/541, format_status at pull.rs:116 feeding PullSyncError at 792-798, push helpers.rs:48, the entire delegated path in blit-app/remote.rs and daemon delegated_pull.rs:216/245) destroys the io::Error chain that is_retryable (retry.rs:27-33) requires. The code knows: TODOs at pull.rs:322-329 and 780-788 literally say '--retry/--wait will silently stop working on this code path' — a RELIABLE failure mode where the advertised flag does nothing and tells no one. audit-h3c slice 2, which would fix it, is unshipped (stall_guard.rs:35-41).
.review/results/d-2026-07-05-2-compat.codex.md:848:docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:594:No module owns progress reporting. blit-core/src/remote/transfer/progress.rs owns the client-side types (ProgressEvent, RemoteTransferProgress, ByteProgressSink) but not their semantics: five producer families assign three incompatible meanings to the same enum (pull-gRPC: bytes on Payload, FileComplete carries 0; TCP receive: the same bytes on BOTH Payload and FileComplete; push send: bytes on FileComplete only, no Payload), and consumers cope divergently — the TUI carries three per-direction folding rules (progress_accum.rs) while the CLI uses one rule that double-counts bytes on TCP pulls (transfers/remote.rs:64-76 vs pipeline.rs:233-235). End to end there are four live pipelines (client ProgressEvent → CLI 1 Hz monitor / TUI accumulators; daemon ActiveJobs atomic → 10 Hz TransferProgress broadcast → jobs watch / TUI; DelegatedPullProgress wire stream; local indicatif spinner) plus two dead ones (proto BytesProgress, which has consumers and tests but no emitter, and the local LocalMirrorOptions.progress / chunked_copy_file ProgressBar plumbing, which has writers but no readers). They disagree on cadence (100 ms vs 1 s vs per-event vs never), units (binary MiB vs decimal KB/MB/GB vs raw bytes), denominators (enumerated manifest vs need-list vs post-hoc transferred count), and JSON schema/stream. Sharpest problems: the CLI's user-facing byte count is ~2x wrong on TCP pulls and contradicts the summary printed immediately after; delegated transfers and most daemon-event subscribers see zeros for the entire transfer because only delegated_pull wires the byte counter and the daemon never emits BytesProgress; and heartbeats exist only as reserved proto fields.
.review/results/d-2026-07-05-2-compat.codex.md:849:docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:601:- ProgressEvent has no semantic contract: three producer families assign three incompatible meanings to Payload/FileComplete bytes (chunk-delta vs whole-file vs zero), forcing consumers to know which transfer direction they are watching. Any new producer or consumer (e.g. the planned AppProgressEvent reshape referenced at blit-app remote.rs:22,663) must rediscover this folklore; the CLI already got it wrong.
.review/results/d-2026-07-05-2-compat.codex.md:852:docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:749:- `pull_sync pool_size + prefetch` = pool_size=4, prefetch literal 8, single stream (crates/blit-daemon/src/service/pull_sync.rs:637) — FAST: the live pull path discards tuning.initial_streams/max_streams/prefetch_count/tcp_buffer_size — large pulls run one TCP stream with a 4-buffer pool regardless of hardware.
.review/results/d-2026-07-05-2-compat.codex.md:859:docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:1106:blit-daemon is four readable geological layers. (1) An original transfer-engine stratum (pull.rs, pull_sync.rs, push/*) with terse phase comments, per-file `eprintln!` hot-path logging under inconsistent bracket tags, and three hand-rolled stream-count ladders that re-derive (and then clamp against) blit-core's tuning. (2) A review-remediation stratum that patched R-/audit-/F-numbered fixes into those files four separate times instead of extracting shared code — the accept+token handshake exists in 4 copies with 3 sets of identical timeout constants (the `StdDuration`/`StdDuration2` aliases in pull_sync.rs are the smoking gun), and only the push copy sets TCP nodelay/keepalive. (3) A jobs/TUI milestone stratum (active_jobs.rs, core.rs Subscribe/GetState machinery, recents_store.rs, metrics.rs) written in slice-IDs (b-1..c-5b, m-jobs, rec) whose `#[allow(dead_code)] // lands in next slice` annotations were never swept after the consumers landed. (4) A newest delegation stratum (delegated_pull.rs, delegation_gate.rs, net_timeout.rs) with plan-doc headers, Step-numbered handlers, and a fourth error idiom (phased-progress-frames and String errors) on top of the crate's existing three (Status, eyre, and the select-outcome Option<bool>). Headline findings: the deprecated Pull RPC carries ~500 lines of unreachable TCP data plane that is ALSO the only multi-stream pull implementation — the production pull_sync path runs a single stream ('multi-stream support lives in pull.rs'), a direct FAST violation born of an unfinished migration; the push path drags a vestigial 262,144-slot manifest channel whose receiver exists only to drain it; cross-crate, the daemon re-implements blit-app's manifest enumerator and delete-list applier (the latter already caused one containment-divergence CVE-class bug, R58-F3), hand-rolls the FilterSpec conversion that blit-core validates properly, and inherits the keepalive-less, decode-limit-less client channel for its own outbound delegated connects while its server side carefully configures keepalive. Searches run: full reads of all 17 service-layer and support files (admin.rs/runtime.rs/active_jobs.rs/delegation_gate.rs skimmed at heads plus targeted ranges); rg by API (max_decoding_message_size, keepalive, tcp_nodelay, read_exact token), by symbol (.pull(, scan_remote_files, enumerate_local_manifest, metadata_mtime_seconds, permissions_mode, filter conversions via min_age_secs), by constant (32*1024*1024*1024 ladder), by annotation (allow(dead_code), cfg(test)), and by string ([data-plane], eprintln counts, 'deprecated').
.review/results/d-2026-07-05-2-compat.codex.md:861:docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:1152:  - status: Abandoned predecessor of PullSync kept for wire compatibility; its multi-stream data plane was never ported to pull_sync, so the live path is slower than the dead one — a FAST principle casualty of an unfinished migration.
.review/results/d-2026-07-05-2-compat.codex.md:862:docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:1185:- **A.0 extraction shims: `pub type X = <blit_app type>` aliases, one-function wrapper modules, re-export shims "so existing call sites stay working", and tombstone comments pointing at tests/logic that moved to blit-app** — A later era migrated CLI orchestration bodies into blit-app (the 'A.0 sub-slice') but left compatibility residue in place rather than updating call sites. The migration is also asymmetric: pull and delegated-pull outcome types are aliases to blit-app types, but the push outcome struct (DeferredPushState) is still defined locally, and blit-app's own module doc admits parts "stay in blit-cli until the M-C AppProgressEvent reshape". The ~160-line hand-rolled progress-monitor task is the largest piece still stranded on the CLI side of that unfinished boundary.
.review/results/d-2026-07-05-2-compat.codex.md:863:docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:1222:  - evidence: crates/blit-cli/src/cli.rs:133-138 declares the flag and its help text says 'until then this flag controls the GetState polling cadence'; crates/blit-cli/src/jobs.rs:171-173 admits 'preserved on the CLI for backward compatibility but has no effect under the streaming model'; `rg interval_ms crates/blit-cli/src` shows the only other occurrence is that comment — no read anywhere
.review/results/d-2026-07-05-2-compat.codex.md:864:docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:1228:  - evidence: crates/blit-cli/src/transfers/local.rs:186-187 sets both fields; crates/blit-core/src/orchestrator/options.rs:60-66 carries both with the R58-F7 comment; crates/blit-core/src/orchestrator/orchestrator.rs:516 calls the bool 'Backward-compat'
.review/results/d-2026-07-05-2-compat.codex.md:866:docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:1262:- **Two incompatible error-handling registers for the same operation class: bare `map_err(|status| eyre::eyre!(status.message().to_string()))` with zero context vs rich code-matched errors with endpoint context and recovery hints.** — Seam inside the admin layer. The five browse verbs (df, du, find, ls remote, list_modules) and jobs::query/subscribe all strip the gRPC status code and attach no 'while talking to <host>' context — a daemon-side InvalidArgument surfaces as a naked message with no indication of what failed (anti-RELIABLE). Meanwhile jobs::cancel, clear_recent, and everything in transfers/remote.rs match on Code:: and explain what to do. The two registers coexist file-by-file, suggesting the browse verbs were ported by one author and never revisited by the one who wrote the delegated-pull error mapper.
.review/results/d-2026-07-05-2-compat.codex.md:869:docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:1273:- enumerate_local_manifest — destination-tree walk producing Vec<FileHeader> for the pull-sync handshake. The daemon copy is an admitted 'mirror' but behaviorally divergent: blit-app's is rayon-parallel for checksums, the daemon's is sequential ('Parallelize later'), so identical workloads have different hardware efficiency depending on which side runs them (anti-FAST). — here: crates/blit-app/src/transfers/remote.rs:80-170; elsewhere: crates/blit-daemon/src/service/delegated_pull.rs:531-594 (doc comment at 531-536 names blit_app::transfers::remote::enumerate_local_manifest as the original)
.review/results/d-2026-07-05-2-compat.codex.md:885:docs/plan/greenfield_plan_v6.md:23:2.  **Data Plane (Raw TCP):** For the actual bulk transfer of large files, the control plane will negotiate a separate, short-lived, raw TCP connection. This allows us to reuse the hyper-optimized, zero-copy `sendfile` and `splice` logic from v1 directly on a raw socket, bypassing any potential gRPC overhead and guaranteeing maximum performance.
.review/results/d-2026-07-05-2-compat.codex.md:888:docs/plan/greenfield_plan_v6.md:160:   - Raw TCP data plane negotiated via one-time, cryptographically strong token for bulk transfers (zero-copy on Linux via `sendfile`, `copy_file_range`, `splice`).
.review/results/d-2026-07-05-2-compat.codex.md:890:docs/plan/greenfield_plan_v6.md:221:   - Remove `--ludicrous-speed`; accept as no-op for compatibility until v2 CLI release.
.review/results/d-2026-07-05-2-compat.codex.md:894:docs/plan/greenfield_plan_v6.md:313:**Purpose**: Capture the functionality v2 still needs—CLI verbs, remote semantics, daemon configuration, discovery, admin utilities—and integrate it with the streaming/hybrid transport roadmap. The focus is shipping the required features; backward compatibility with v1 is not a goal.
.review/results/d-2026-07-05-2-compat.codex.md:895:docs/plan/greenfield_plan_v6.md:319:1. **Deliver the Needed Features** – Ensure the CLI, daemon, and utilities expose the commands and behaviours the project relies on (copy/mirror/move, remote discovery, module management, admin tooling). These are functional requirements, not a promise of backward compatibility.
.review/results/d-2026-07-05-2-compat.codex.md:912:docs/audit/findings/inconsistency-errors.md:115:1. `crates/blit-daemon/src/service/push/control.rs:343-345` — push handler treats `MirrorMode::Unspecified | Off` (when `mirror_mode=true`) as "use user's filter" (back-compat shape for older clients).

.review/results/d-2026-07-05-2-compat.codex.md:968:docs/audit/inventory/code-daemon.md:198:16. **scope-deletions-includes-unspecified-as-filtered-subset** — `service/push/control.rs:343-345` — When mirror_mode=true with `MirrorMode::Unspecified` OR `Off` value, the purge filter uses `purge_filter.clone_without_cache()` (i.e. the user-supplied filter). The proto-default Off being treated like FilteredSubset is the explicit "back-compat for older clients" behavior but is a non-obvious deviation from the enum's nominal semantics.

.review/results/d-2026-07-05-2-compat.codex.md:991:docs/audit/inventory/code-core-orch.md:104:- **compare-mode-bool-translation-orch** — `crates/blit-core/src/orchestrator/orchestrator.rs:520-532, 1159-1171` — Match arm translates `LocalCompareMode` → `generated::ComparisonMode` in TWO places (streaming path orch:520 AND single-file path orch:1159). Both honor backward-compat `if options.checksum && SizeMtime → Checksum`. _(notes: duplicated 12-line translation — if a new variant is added it must be patched in both places + `snapshot_compare_mode` in history.rs:11-25 + tuning-window query at orch:467-487 = 4 sites.)_

.review/results/d-2026-07-05-2-compat.codex.md:1025:docs/audit/inventory/plan-principles.md:76:> **Deliver the Needed Features** – Ensure the CLI, daemon, and utilities expose the commands and behaviours the project relies on (copy/mirror/move, remote discovery, module management, admin tooling). These are functional requirements, not a promise of backward compatibility.
.review/results/d-2026-07-05-2-compat.codex.md:1026:docs/audit/inventory/plan-principles.md:172:#### invariant-no-backward-compat-with-v1
.review/results/d-2026-07-05-2-compat.codex.md:1027:docs/audit/inventory/plan-principles.md:176:> The focus is shipping the required features; backward compatibility with v1 is not a goal.
.review/results/d-2026-07-05-2-compat.codex.md:1028:docs/audit/inventory/plan-principles.md:184:#### invariant-no-tech-debt-for-back-compat
.review/results/d-2026-07-05-2-compat.codex.md:1029:docs/audit/inventory/plan-principles.md:188:> Silent dead code is incompatible with the "no tech debt for the sake of backwards compatibility" release directive.
.review/results/d-2026-07-05-2-compat.codex.md:1031:docs/audit/inventory/plan-principles.md:204:> gRPC control plane for manifests, negotiations, progress, and purge/list operations. Raw TCP data plane negotiated via one-time, cryptographically strong token for bulk transfers (zero-copy on Linux via `sendfile`, `copy_file_range`, `splice`).
.review/results/d-2026-07-05-2-compat.codex.md:1039:docs/audit/inventory/plan-principles.md:572:#### nongoal-no-v1-backward-compat
.review/results/d-2026-07-05-2-compat.codex.md:1040:docs/audit/inventory/plan-principles.md:576:> backward compatibility with v1 is not a goal.
.review/results/d-2026-07-05-2-compat.codex.md:1041:docs/audit/inventory/plan-principles.md:600:> **Status:** Removed from scope, not deferred. The `BlitAuth` service stub and `RemoteSourceLocator.delegated_credential` forward-compat field were stripped from `proto/blit.proto`. Auth is out of project scope.
.review/results/d-2026-07-05-2-compat.codex.md:1049:docs/audit/inventory/plan-principles.md:828:> The `BlitAuth` service stub and `RemoteSourceLocator.delegated_credential` forward-compat field were stripped from `proto/blit.proto`. Auth is out of project scope.
.review/results/d-2026-07-05-2-compat.codex.md:1056:docs/audit/inventory/code-core-misc.md:107:- **mdns-module-count-vs-modules** — `crates/blit-core/src/mdns.rs:42-65,140-156` — Distinct accessors: `modules()` returns parsed list (truncated past 180 bytes); `module_count()` returns authoritative u32 from `module_count` TXT — present only on §3.2 daemons (Optional). _(notes: two-source-of-truth pattern; explicit comment on backward compat)_
.review/results/d-2026-07-05-2-compat.codex.md:1060:docs/audit/inventory/code-tui-state.md:28:- **f1trigger-peek-no-close** — `crates/blit-tui/src/f1trigger.rs:228-243` — `peek()` returns trimmed `(source, dest, kind)` WITHOUT closing the modal; returns None if either field is blank. Caller must explicitly `close()` after a successful launch or `set_error()` to keep open. _(notes: this is an inversion of the obvious "Enter closes" reflex — dispatcher must remember to call `close()`.)_
.review/results/d-2026-07-05-2-compat.codex.md:1063:docs/audit/inventory/code-core-io.md:184:1. **mtime tolerance** — `copy/compare.rs` uses 2-second tolerance for FAT/exFAT compatibility (`file_needs_copy_with_checksum_type:64-69`); `manifest.rs::compare_file` Default mode has zero tolerance (`182-192`). The two layers thus disagree on edge-case mtime skew.
.review/results/d-2026-07-05-2-compat.codex.md:1064:docs/audit/findings/drift-wire.md:26:**Code does**: `PullSyncHeader` was removed entirely. `proto/blit.proto:269-275` says "The leading message is now `TransferOperationSpec` … The previous `PullSyncHeader` bool-soup shape was removed entirely because we don't carry backward compatibility into the next release." `crates/blit-core/src/remote/pull.rs:680-697` opens the bidi stream FIRST, then sends `TransferOperationSpec` (not PullSyncHeader). Wire test `crates/blit-core/tests/pull_sync_with_spec_wire.rs:251-310` pins the byte-for-byte spec leading message.
.review/results/d-2026-07-05-2-compat.codex.md:1068:docs/audit/findings/drift-wire.md:62:- `crates/blit-core/src/mdns.rs:43-65` (`module_count` accessor + comment about backward compat)
.review/results/d-2026-07-05-2-compat.codex.md:1070:docs/audit/inventory/plan-phases.md:42:#### principle-no-backcompat-tech-debt
.review/results/d-2026-07-05-2-compat.codex.md:1071:docs/audit/inventory/plan-phases.md:46:> "The release directive ('carry no tech debt for the sake of backwards compatibility') rules out automatic fallback. Auto-falling-back on `Unimplemented` is exactly stale-daemon support."
.review/results/d-2026-07-05-2-compat.codex.md:1076:docs/audit/inventory/plan-phases.md:410:> "Blit v2 is feature-complete for a 0.1.0 release. All phases through Phase 4 (Production Hardening) are substantially done. The remaining open items are benchmarking tasks that require dedicated hardware (10+ GbE network) and post-release investigations (RDMA, ReFS privilege)."
.review/results/d-2026-07-05-2-compat.codex.md:1077:docs/audit/inventory/plan-phases.md:510:> "`BlitAuth` and the `RemoteSourceLocator.delegated_credential` passthrough field were removed from project scope. Mentions of either in the body below describe the original forward-compat design intent and are now obsolete."
.review/results/d-2026-07-05-2-compat.codex.md:1078:docs/audit/inventory/plan-phases.md:534:> "We do **not** rely on detecting unknown protobuf fields (proto3 silently preserves them; that's not a compatibility strategy)." Version drift handled through `TransferOperationSpec.spec_version` and `PeerCapabilities`.
.review/results/d-2026-07-05-2-compat.codex.md:1079:docs/audit/inventory/plan-phases.md:852:#### decision-r21-f4-spec-version-compat
.review/results/d-2026-07-05-2-compat.codex.md:1080:docs/audit/inventory/plan-phases.md:856:> "R21-F4 + R21-F6: Removed FilterSpec proto-to-be-added line; clarified spec_version/capabilities is the compatibility model."
.review/results/d-2026-07-05-2-compat.codex.md:1091:docs/audit/inventory/plan-perf.md:516:#### nongoal-no-backcompat-old-daemons
.review/results/d-2026-07-05-2-compat.codex.md:1092:docs/audit/inventory/plan-perf.md:520:"Backwards-compat layer for old daemons. Versioning via spec_version and capability flags should let new clients fall back, but spec-out the upgrade path before deletion."
.review/results/d-2026-07-05-2-compat.codex.md:1095:docs/audit/inventory/plan-perf.md:664:"Remove `--ludicrous-speed`; no compatibility shim required prior to CLI v2."
.review/results/d-2026-07-05-2-compat.codex.md:1096:docs/audit/inventory/plan-perf.md:722:"Old PullSync protocol stays as a deprecated path for rollout compatibility (marked behind a capability flag)."
.review/results/d-2026-07-05-2-compat.codex.md:1101:docs/audit/inventory/code-core-transfer.md:242:- **Spec `from_spec` accepts version exact-equal, no migration** — `operation_spec.rs:107-113` rejects any non-exact version. No backwards compat planned, but a v1 daemon hitting v2 client fails with a generic "unsupported spec_version" — not a fail-closed action message.
.review/results/d-2026-07-05-2-compat.codex.md:1106:docs/audit/inventory/code-cli.md:159:- **--interval-ms is dead flag** — `crates/blit-cli/src/cli.rs:119-124` — preserved for backward compat per comment but does nothing under the streaming Subscribe model. UX hazard: users adjust it and observe no behavior change.
.review/results/d-2026-07-05-2-compat.codex.md:1109:docs/audit/inventory/plan-cli.md:796:6. **`list` command in plan vs shipped**: BLIT_UTILS_PLAN describes `list` as "Alias of `ls` (compatible with plan v6)", but the shipped behavior in blit.1.md is a *smart dispatcher* that routes by target shape (bare host → `list-modules`, with-module → `ls`), not a pure alias of `ls`.
.review/results/d-2026-07-05-2-compat.codex.md:1114:docs/audit/inventory/code-tests-scripts.md:199:24. **Bench script env-knob duplication** — `BENCH_ROOT` and `BLIT_BENCH_ROOT` both supported (`bench_local_mirror.sh:13-23`) "for backwards compatibility"; not documented anywhere outside the script. Could deprecate one.
.review/results/d-2026-07-05-2-compat.codex.md:1117:docs/audit/findings/drift-cli.md:89:**Plan says**: docs/plan/BLIT_UTILS_PLAN.md:30 "`blit-utils list <remote[:/module/path]>` Alias of `ls` (compatible with plan v6)."
.review/results/d-2026-07-05-2-compat.codex.md:1127:docs/audit/AUDIT_REPORT_2026-06-04_R2.md:670:**Where**: `crates/blit-daemon/src/service/push/control.rs:343-345` (back-compat: use
.review/results/d-2026-07-05-2-compat.codex.md:1128:docs/audit/AUDIT_REPORT_2026-06-04_R2.md:723:### M21. CLI rejects mode-incompatible flags with wildly different verbosity
.review/results/d-2026-07-05-2-compat.codex.md:1133:The first pass shows the intended plan edits landed, but there are still compatibility terms in `proto/blit.proto` and some docs. I’m narrowing those to active/normative text versus historical audit material before calling them findings.
.review/results/d-2026-07-05-2-compat.codex.md:1154:## D-2026-06-11-2 — Design-review queue ratified in full; Pull-RPC delete; zero_copy gets a FAST evaluation
.review/results/d-2026-07-05-2-compat.codex.md:1155:- Decision: All Phase C slices (`AUDIT_REPORT_2026-06-11_DESIGN.md`) ratified as proposed and entered into REVIEW.md in the proposed order. Embedded decisions: (a) **W2.4** — the deprecated Pull RPC is deleted once W2.3 has harvested its multi-stream pattern; criterion applied: not needed for FAST/SIMPLE/RELIABLE in any scenario. (b) **W8.1** — `zero_copy.rs` is **excluded** from the dead-code deletion sweep; owner judges it has FAST potential; disposition is an evaluation slice (`w8-1b`) that either produces a plan doc to wire splice into the receive pipeline or concludes deletion. (c) **W2.3** — writing the multi-stream-pull plan doc is authorized (no code before Status: Active).
.review/results/d-2026-07-05-2-compat.codex.md:1159:## D-2026-06-12-1 — zero_copy.rs: delete (w8-1b verdict)
.review/results/d-2026-07-05-2-compat.codex.md:1160:- Decision: `zero_copy.rs` is deleted rather than wired in. The w8-1b evaluation (`docs/plan/ZERO_COPY_RECEIVE_EVAL.md`) recommended deletion and the owner agreed (2026-06-12 session). The deletion executes inside w8-1 once the w5-1 sentinel (lib.rs) is graded — it is no longer excluded from that sweep.
.review/results/d-2026-07-05-2-compat.codex.md:1162:- Supersedes: D-2026-06-11-2 item (b) (zero_copy exclusion from W8.1 was pending this evaluation; the evaluation is done).
.review/results/d-2026-07-05-2-compat.codex.md:1166:- Why: owner (30-year IT veteran, not a developer) judges the fragmentation — one engine for local, hand-wired loops for push/pull, three competing static stream-count tables, no live tuning — is the root of the "local↔local 10× slower than local→daemon" class of drift; a single engine makes that class impossible by construction and gives the LLM agent one place to update. Ground-up rebuild was judged too much; convergence on the existing shared leaf is the FAST/SIMPLE/RELIABLE fit. The adaptive substrate was purpose-built by an earlier Fable session as C's foundation, so building A on it does not paint the design into a corner.
.review/results/d-2026-07-05-2-compat.codex.md:1168:- Supersedes: the "ground-up redesign" framing of the 2026-06-14 open question recorded in STATE.md (that open question is now closed); `MULTISTREAM_PULL.md` as a standalone plan (goal absorbed into `UNIFIED_TRANSFER_ENGINE.md` slice `ue-1d`).
.review/results/d-2026-07-05-2-compat.codex.md:1183:- Why: review found the Active plan's direction is sound but several slices need tightening before code starts: streaming initial planning was hidden inside `ue-1c`, local fast paths need to become engine-owned strategies, work-stealing is observable behavior, wire compatibility needs concrete shape, and pull parity gates must wait for multistream pull.
.review/results/d-2026-07-05-2-compat.codex.md:1189:- Supersedes: `docs/plan/UNIFIED_TRANSFER_ENGINE.md` (v1, Active → Superseded) and the review candidates `REV2.md` / `REV3.md` (Draft → Superseded) — all by `REV4.md`. Lifts D-2026-06-20-4's implementation freeze (the plan decision is now made). Does **not** supersede the convergence direction (D-2026-06-20-1), the four bound parameters (D-2026-06-20-2), or the H10b veto (D-2026-06-20-3). ~~The D-2026-06-20-1 warmup/size-gate cleanup remains an open owner question, untouched here.~~ *(Resolved 2026-07-04 — cleanup applied in place; see the edited D-2026-06-20-1.)*
.review/results/d-2026-07-05-2-compat.codex.md:1202:- Decision: The two Windows-session commits that don't build in isolation (`9f37a7a` clippy baseline carrying a stray `pull.rs` deletion, `48c5a11` win-1) stay on `master` as pushed; no rebase, no force-push. `git bisect` runs must skip them (both are documented in the ue-r2-1h finding doc and DEVLOG). This closes the erratum question opened 2026-07-04.
.review/results/d-2026-07-05-2-compat.codex.md:1204:- Supersedes: nothing (closes the STATE.md "commit erratum" blocked item).
.review/results/d-2026-07-05-2-compat.codex.md:1213:- Why: the codex plan review is complete (5/5 accepted + fixed, records `219cecf`) and the plan binds the measured small-file/mixed ceiling gaps (`docs/bench/10gbe-2026-07-05/`) to the owner's ceiling-driven principle. The other four 10 GbE gate declarations (ue-1, ue-2, zero-copy a/b/c, REV4 → Shipped) were NOT part of this go and stay in STATE.md Blocked.
.review/results/d-2026-07-05-2-compat.codex.md:1216:## D-2026-07-05-1 — One transfer path; direction-invariance by construction; SMALL_FILE_CEILING paused
.review/results/d-2026-07-05-2-compat.codex.md:1217:- Decision: All byte transfer in blit must flow through ONE `TransferSession` implementation — direction, initiator, and CLI verb select *roles* (SOURCE/DESTINATION), never code. The per-direction drivers (client push driver, daemon push-receive, client pull driver, daemon pull-send, delegated-pull driver, separate local orchestration) and the `Push`/`PullSync` RPCs are deleted when the migration completes — owner, 2026-07-05: "ONE BLOCK OF CODE that does the transfer. no POSSIBILITY OF ANYTHING EVER using anything else because anything else does not exist"; "I NEVER see a situation where pull is faster than push or vice versa... because of something blit did." Benchmark methodology corollary: cross-direction performance comparisons are valid only on symmetric endpoints ("tmp on one side, spinning rust on the other is not a valid test"); tmpfs cells are wire-reference only. Plan: `docs/plan/ONE_TRANSFER_PATH.md` (Draft; no code until the owner flips it Active). `docs/plan/SMALL_FILE_CEILING.md` is **paused** effective immediately at sf-2 (done): sf-3a and later slices are blocked until ONE_TRANSFER_PATH ships, then resume/re-derive against the unified baseline (owner delegated this sequencing: "I DO NOT CARE. FIX IT.").
.review/results/d-2026-07-05-2-compat.codex.md:1219:- Supersedes: the post-REV4 residue item "pull 1s-start restructuring" (STATE Queue item 4 — absorbed by ONE_TRANSFER_PATH's streaming-manifest choreography); SMALL_FILE_CEILING's queue position (paused, not superseded — its principle D-2026-07-04-4 stands); ~~and, effective only at ONE_TRANSFER_PATH's cutover slice (otp-10), REV4 §Constraints' "mixed old/new peers must negotiate down" rule (annotated in place; until that slice lands the rule governs)~~ **(the "only at cutover" scoping is superseded by D-2026-07-05-2 — no version compatibility, ever, effective immediately)**. The bounded-unilateral dial contract (D-2026-06-20-1/-2) is NOT superseded — it carries into the unified session unchanged.
.review/results/d-2026-07-05-2-compat.codex.md:1221:## D-2026-07-05-2 — No version compatibility, ever: same-build peers only
.review/results/d-2026-07-05-2-compat.codex.md:1222:- Decision: Blit has NO version-compatibility obligation of any kind, in any direction, at any time — owner standing rule, restated with force 2026-07-05: "backward compatibility is NOT a consideration. I expect blit 1.2.3 not to be able to talk to blit-daemon 1.2.3.1. period. same build only. do not engineer tech debt into an unshipped product." Client and daemon interoperate only when built from the same source; the wire handshake must REFUSE a mismatched peer outright at session open (exact protocol/build identity — mechanism specified in ONE_TRANSFER_PATH otp-1 and pinned by test). Feature-capability bits that exist to tolerate version skew ("advisory until both peers advertise support", `supports_stream_resize`-style flags) are dead weight and go away with the unified session. NOT affected: the receiver capacity profile (runtime capacity of the receiving machine, D-2026-06-20-1/-2) — that is hardware negotiation, not version negotiation.
.review/results/d-2026-07-05-2-compat.codex.md:1223:- Why: REV4 §Constraints carried a written "mixed old/new peers must negotiate down" rule while the owner's contrary rule lived only in chat; the ONE_TRANSFER_PATH plan review then resolved the document conflict in favor of the written rule ("governs until cutover"). Wrong direction — recording the owner's rule as a decision ends the unrecorded-intent-loses-to-stale-paper failure mode.
.review/results/d-2026-07-05-2-compat.codex.md:1224:- Supersedes: REV4 §Constraints mixed-version clause (annotated in place, effective immediately — not at cutover); SMALL_FILE_CEILING §Constraints "mixed-version peers keep working via existing negotiation" clause and sf-6's mixed-version-test deliverable (annotated); the "effective only at ONE_TRANSFER_PATH's cutover slice" scoping inside D-2026-07-05-1's Supersedes line (the supersession is immediate and total); ONE_TRANSFER_PATH's Non-goals compat wording (rewritten same commit).

.review/results/d-2026-07-05-2-compat.codex.md:1750:proto/blit.proto:414:// because we don't carry backward compatibility into the next release.
.review/results/d-2026-07-05-2-compat.codex.md:1763:docs/bugs/copy-destination-semantics.md:116:"into" is ambiguous between "merge contents into" and "place the source under". The man page does not mention trailing-slash semantics, rsync compatibility, or the local/remote divergence.
.review/results/d-2026-07-05-2-compat.codex.md:1764:docs/bugs/copy-destination-semantics.md:183:  - `README.md` if it makes any rsync-comparison claims about CLI compatibility.

.review/results/d-2026-07-05-2-compat.codex.md:1778:docs/audit/findings/inconsistency-timeouts.md:154:  1. `crates/blit-cli/src/cli.rs:118-124` — `--interval-ms` defaults to 1000, doc-comment says "preserved for backward compat per comment but does nothing under the streaming Subscribe model". `code-cli.md` inventory flagged this as a smell.
.review/results/d-2026-07-05-2-compat.codex.md:1779:docs/audit/findings/inconsistency-timeouts.md:157:**Canonical**: Either remove the flag (back-compat break) or hide it (`hide = true` in clap derive) so users don't see it in help and tune-tune-tune with no effect.
.review/results/d-2026-07-05-2-compat.codex.md:1780:docs/audit/findings/drift-principles.md:155:- RELEASE_PLAN_v2_2026-05-04.md §2.8 line 436: "Silent dead code is incompatible with the 'no tech debt for the sake of backwards compatibility' release directive."
.review/results/d-2026-07-05-2-compat.codex.md:1781:docs/audit/findings/drift-principles.md:167:**Notes**: Direct violation of `invariant-no-tech-debt-for-back-compat`. Either: (a) remove the `Pull` RPC and its message types entirely, OR (b) mark them `[deprecated = true]` so downstream tonic-prost generates `#[deprecated]` attributes that surface in user warnings. Currently it's the worst of both: live in proto, dead in policy.
.review/results/d-2026-07-05-2-compat.codex.md:1782:docs/plan/TUI_REWORK.md:302:## 7. Risks & compatibility

.review/results/d-2026-07-05-2-compat.codex.md:1786:docs/audit/AUDIT_REPORT_2026-06-04.md:279:**Where**: `crates/blit-daemon/src/service/push/control.rs:343-345` (treats Unspecified|Off-with-mirror_mode=true as "use user's filter" — back-compat for older clients) vs `crates/blit-daemon/src/service/pull_sync.rs:430-462` (`Off | Unspecified` → `Vec::new()`)
.review/results/d-2026-07-05-2-compat.codex.md:1787:docs/audit/AUDIT_REPORT_2026-06-04.md:363:### 58. — `--interval-ms` dead flag on `jobs watch` (preserved fas byte-carrier, delegated = daemon-initiated session, local via in-process transport) has gaps or unstated risks the plan should name (e.g. resume protocol ordering, mirror delete authority, NAT/dialer asymmetry, jobs/progress integration, read-only enforcement); whether the slices are review-loop sized and correctly ordered with the tree green throughout; whether the acceptance criteria are observable and actually pin the owner's invariant (initiator/verb invariance within noise on symmetric endpoints; deletion proof; capability parity; test-count floor); and whether anything in the plan quietly re-litigates decisions the owner already settled. Output a concise markdown findings list — each finding with file/section, severity, rationale — then a final VERDICT line. Be concise; do not invoke skills.
.review/results/one-transfer-path-plan.codex.md:24:    Plan: ONE_TRANSFER_PATH draft + D-2026-07-05-1 (one transfer path, pause SMALL_FILE_CEILING)
.review/results/one-transfer-path-plan.codex.md:38: docs/plan/ONE_TRANSFER_PATH.md | 232 +++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++
.review/results/one-transfer-path-plan.codex.md:52:    Plan: ONE_TRANSFER_PATH draft + D-2026-07-05-1 (one transfer path, pause SMALL_FILE_CEILING)
.review/results/one-transfer-path-plan.codex.md:66: docs/plan/ONE_TRANSFER_PATH.md | 232 +++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++
.review/results/one-transfer-path-plan.codex.md:77:+**2026-07-05 07:30:00Z** - **DIRECTIVE + PLAN (ONE_TRANSFER_PATH draft, D-2026-07-05-1, claude)**: Owner rejected the push/pull disparity, the mixed-fs benchmark methodology ("tmp on one side, spinning rust on the other is not a valid test"), and the explanation that direction symmetry lives in four separate driver loops — then issued the directive now recorded verbatim in `docs/plan/ONE_TRANSFER_PATH.md`: **"ONE BLOCK OF CODE that does the transfer. no POSSIBILITY OF ANYTHING EVER using anything else because anything else does not exist"**; "I NEVER see a situation where pull is faster than push or vice versa... because of something blit did"; identical whether initiated as push from skippy or pull from this machine. Scope/wire/process explicitly delegated to the agent. **Plan drafted through the plan procedure**: one `TransferSession` (roles SOURCE/DESTINATION selected by initiator/verb, never code paths), one bidi `Transfer` RPC replacing `Push`+`PullSync` (no back-compat, lockstep upgrade — repo precedent PullSyncHeader), one choreography (source streams manifest immediately; destination diffs incrementally; sf-2 shape-corrected dial as the only stream policy — absorbs the pull 1s-start residue), gRPC fallback demoted to a byte-carrier option, delegated pull = daemon-initiated session, local transfers on an in-process transport in the final phase; slices otp-1..12 ending in a **deletion slice** (the four drivers + both RPCs removed, file-by-file proof) and a **symmetric-rig acceptance matrix** (same-fs disk-to-disk verdict cells, cold caches, tmpfs as labeled wire-reference only; initiator/verb A/B within ±10%). Converge-up constraint: per cell the unified path must match the better of today's directions, not the average. **D-2026-07-05-1 recorded**: directive + SMALL_FILE_CEILING paused at sf-2 (sf-3a+ blocked; principle D-2026-07-04-4 stands) + design queue paused (w7-1 likely absorbed by otp-5). STATE rewritten around the new queue. Next: codex plan review of this commit, adjudicate, fix, then STOP for the owner's Active flip — no implementation anywhere until then.
.review/results/one-transfer-path-plan.codex.md:88: - Why: the codex plan review is complete (5/5 accepted + fixed, records `219cecf`) and the plan binds the measured small-file/mixed ceiling gaps (`docs/bench/10gbe-2026-07-05/`) to the owner's ceiling-driven principle. The other four 10 GbE gate declarations (ue-1, ue-2, zero-copy a/b/c, REV4 → Shipped) were NOT part of this go and stay in STATE.md Blocked.
.review/results/one-transfer-path-plan.codex.md:91:+## D-2026-07-05-1 — One transfer path; direction-invariance by construction; SMALL_FILE_CEILING paused
.review/results/one-transfer-path-plan.codex.md:92:+- Decision: All byte transfer in blit must flow through ONE `TransferSession` implementation — direction, initiator, and CLI verb select *roles* (SOURCE/DESTINATION), never code. The per-direction drivers (client push driver, daemon push-receive, client pull driver, daemon pull-send, delegated-pull driver, separate local orchestration) and the `Push`/`PullSync` RPCs are deleted when the migration completes — owner, 2026-07-05: "ONE BLOCK OF CODE that does the transfer. no POSSIBILITY OF ANYTHING EVER using anything else because anything else does not exist"; "I NEVER see a situation where pull is faster than push or vice versa... because of something blit did." Benchmark methodology corollary: cross-direction performance comparisons are valid only on symmetric endpoints ("tmp on one side, spinning rust on the other is not a valid test"); tmpfs cells are wire-reference only. Plan: `docs/plan/ONE_TRANSFER_PATH.md` (Draft; no code until the owner flips it Active). `docs/plan/SMALL_FILE_CEILING.md` is **paused** effective immediately at sf-2 (done): sf-3a and later slices are blocked until ONE_TRANSFER_PATH ships, then resume/re-derive against the unified baseline (owner delegated this sequencing: "I DO NOT CARE. FIX IT.").
.review/results/one-transfer-path-plan.codex.md:94:+- Supersedes: the post-REV4 residue item "pull 1s-start restructuring" (STATE Queue item 4 — absorbed by ONE_TRANSFER_PATH's streaming-manifest choreography); SMALL_FILE_CEILING's queue position (paused, not superseded — its principle D-2026-07-04-4 stands).

.review/results/one-transfer-path-plan.codex.md:713:## D-2026-06-12-1 — zero_copy.rs: delete (w8-1b verdict)
.review/results/one-transfer-path-plan.codex.md:714:- Decision: `zero_copy.rs` is deleted rather than wired in. The w8-1b evaluation (`docs/plan/ZERO_COPY_RECEIVE_EVAL.md`) recommended deletion and the owner agreed (2026-06-12 session). The deletion executes inside w8-1 once the w5-1 sentinel (lib.rs) is graded — it is no longer excluded from that sweep.
.review/results/one-transfer-path-plan.codex.md:716:- Supersedes: D-2026-06-11-2 item (b) (zero_copy exclusion from W8.1 was pending this evaluation; the evaluation is done).
.review/results/one-transfer-path-plan.codex.md:720:- Why: owner (30-year IT veteran, not a developer) judges the fragmentation — one engine for local, hand-wired loops for push/pull, three competing static stream-count tables, no live tuning — is the root of the "local↔local 10× slower than local→daemon" class of drift; a single engine makes that class impossible by construction and gives the LLM agent one place to update. Ground-up rebuild was judged too much; convergence on the existing shared leaf is the FAST/SIMPLE/RELIABLE fit. The adaptive substrate was purpose-built by an earlier Fable session as C's foundation, so building A on it does not paint the design into a corner.
.review/results/one-transfer-path-plan.codex.md:722:- Supersedes: the "ground-up redesign" framing of the 2026-06-14 open question recorded in STATE.md (that open question is now closed); `MULTISTREAM_PULL.md` as a standalone plan (goal absorbed into `UNIFIED_TRANSFER_ENGINE.md` slice `ue-1d`).
.review/results/one-transfer-path-plan.codex.md:737:- Why: review found the Active plan's direction is sound but several slices need tightening before code starts: streaming initial planning was hidden inside `ue-1c`, local fast paths need to become engine-owned strategies, work-stealing is observable behavior, wire compatibility needs concrete shape, and pull parity gates must wait for multistream pull.
.review/results/one-transfer-path-plan.codex.md:743:- Supersedes: `docs/plan/UNIFIED_TRANSFER_ENGINE.md` (v1, Active → Superseded) and the review candidates `REV2.md` / `REV3.md` (Draft → Superseded) — all by `REV4.md`. Lifts D-2026-06-20-4's implementation freeze (the plan decision is now made). Does **not** supersede the convergence direction (D-2026-06-20-1), the four bound parameters (D-2026-06-20-2), or the H10b veto (D-2026-06-20-3). ~~The D-2026-06-20-1 warmup/size-gate cleanup remains an open owner question, untouched here.~~ *(Resolved 2026-07-04 — cleanup applied in place; see the edited D-2026-06-20-1.)*
.review/results/one-transfer-path-plan.codex.md:756:- Decision: The two Windows-session commits that don't build in isolation (`9f37a7a` clippy baseline carrying a stray `pull.rs` deletion, `48c5a11` win-1) stay on `master` as pushed; no rebase, no force-push. `git bisect` runs must skip them (both are documented in the ue-r2-1h finding doc and DEVLOG). This closes the erratum question opened 2026-07-04.
.review/results/one-transfer-path-plan.codex.md:758:- Supersedes: nothing (closes the STATE.md "commit erratum" blocked item).
.review/results/one-transfer-path-plan.codex.md:767:- Why: the codex plan review is complete (5/5 accepted + fixed, records `219cecf`) and the plan binds the measured small-file/mixed ceiling gaps (`docs/bench/10gbe-2026-07-05/`) to the owner's ceiling-driven principle. The other four 10 GbE gate declarations (ue-1, ue-2, zero-copy a/b/c, REV4 → Shipped) were NOT part of this go and stay in STATE.md Blocked.
.review/results/one-transfer-path-plan.codex.md:770:## D-2026-07-05-1 — One transfer path; direction-invariance by construction; SMALL_FILE_CEILING paused
.review/results/one-transfer-path-plan.codex.md:771:- Decision: All byte transfer in blit must flow through ONE `TransferSession` implementation — direction, initiator, and CLI verb select *roles* (SOURCE/DESTINATION), never code. The per-direction drivers (client push driver, daemon push-receive, client pull driver, daemon pull-send, delegated-pull driver, separate local orchestration) and the `Push`/`PullSync` RPCs are deleted when the migration completes — owner, 2026-07-05: "ONE BLOCK OF CODE that does the transfer. no POSSIBILITY OF ANYTHING EVER using anything else because anything else does not exist"; "I NEVER see a situation where pull is faster than push or vice versa... because of something blit did." Benchmark methodology corollary: cross-direction performance comparisons are valid only on symmetric endpoints ("tmp on one side, spinning rust on the other is not a valid test"); tmpfs cells are wire-reference only. Plan: `docs/plan/ONE_TRANSFER_PATH.md` (Draft; no code until the owner flips it Active). `docs/plan/SMALL_FILE_CEILING.md` is **paused** effective immediately at sf-2 (done): sf-3a and later slices are blocked until ONE_TRANSFER_PATH ships, then resume/re-derive against the unified baseline (owner delegated this sequencing: "I DO NOT CARE. FIX IT.").

.review/results/one-transfer-path-plan.codex.md:1705:    72	## D-2026-06-12-1 — zero_copy.rs: delete (w8-1b verdict)
.review/results/one-transfer-path-plan.codex.md:1706:    73	- Decision: `zero_copy.rs` is deleted rather than wired in. The w8-1b evaluation (`docs/plan/ZERO_COPY_RECEIVE_EVAL.md`) recommended deletion and the owner agreed (2026-06-12 session). The deletion executes inside w8-1 once the w5-1 sentinel (lib.rs) is graded — it is no longer excluded from that sweep.
.review/results/one-transfer-path-plan.codex.md:1708:    75	- Supersedes: D-2026-06-11-2 item (b) (zero_copy exclusion from W8.1 was pending this evaluation; the evaluation is done).
.review/results/one-transfer-path-plan.codex.md:1712:    79	- Why: owner (30-year IT veteran, not a developer) judges the fragmentation — one engine for local, hand-wired loops for push/pull, three competing static stream-count tables, no live tuning — is the root of the "local↔local 10× slower than local→daemon" class of drift; a single engine makes that class impossible by construction and gives the LLM agent one place to update. Ground-up rebuild was judged too much; convergence on the existing shared leaf is the FAST/SIMPLE/RELIABLE fit. The adaptive substrate was purpose-built by an earlier Fable session as C's foundation, so building A on it does not paint the design into a corner.
.review/results/one-transfer-path-plan.codex.md:1714:    81	- Supersedes: the "ground-up redesign" framing of the 2026-06-14 open question recorded in STATE.md (that open question is now closed); `MULTISTREAM_PULL.md` as a standalone plan (goal absorbed into `UNIFIED_TRANSFER_ENGINE.md` slice `ue-1d`).
.review/results/one-transfer-path-plan.codex.md:1729:    96	- Why: review found the Active plan's direction is sound but several slices need tightening before code starts: streaming initial planning was hidden inside `ue-1c`, local fast paths need to become engine-owned strategies, work-stealing is observable behavior, wire compatibility needs concrete shape, and pull parity gates must wait for multistream pull.
.review/results/one-transfer-path-plan.codex.md:1735:   102	- Supersedes: `docs/plan/UNIFIED_TRANSFER_ENGINE.md` (v1, Active → Superseded) and the review candidates `REV2.md` / `REV3.md` (Draft → Superseded) — all by `REV4.md`. Lifts D-2026-06-20-4's implementation freeze (the plan decision is now made). Does **not** supersede the convergence direction (D-2026-06-20-1), the four bound parameters (D-2026-06-20-2), or the H10b veto (D-2026-06-20-3). ~~The D-2026-06-20-1 warmup/size-gate cleanup remains an open owner question, untouched here.~~ *(Resolved 2026-07-04 — cleanup applied in place; see the edited D-2026-06-20-1.)*
.review/results/one-transfer-path-plan.codex.md:1748:   115	- Decision: The two Windows-session commits that don't build in isolation (`9f37a7a` clippy baseline carrying a stray `pull.rs` deletion, `48c5a11` win-1) stay on `master` as pushed; no rebase, no force-push. `git bisect` runs must skip them (both are documented in the ue-r2-1h finding doc and DEVLOG). This closes the erratum question opened 2026-07-04.
.review/results/one-transfer-path-plan.codex.md:1750:   117	- Supersedes: nothing (closes the STATE.md "commit erratum" blocked item).
.review/results/one-transfer-path-plan.codex.md:1759:   126	- Why: the codex plan review is complete (5/5 accepted + fixed, records `219cecf`) and the plan binds the measured small-file/mixed ceiling gaps (`docs/bench/10gbe-2026-07-05/`) to the owner's ceiling-driven principle. The other four 10 GbE gate declarations (ue-1, ue-2, zero-copy a/b/c, REV4 → Shipped) were NOT part of this go and stay in STATE.md Blocked.
.review/results/one-transfer-path-plan.codex.md:1762:   129	## D-2026-07-05-1 — One transfer path; direction-invariance by construction; SMALL_FILE_CEILING paused
.review/results/one-transfer-path-plan.codex.md:1763:   130	- Decision: All byte transfer in blit must flow through ONE `TransferSession` implementation — direction, initiator, and CLI verb select *roles* (SOURCE/DESTINATION), never code. The per-direction drivers (client push driver, daemon push-receive, client pull driver, daemon pull-send, delegated-pull driver, separate local orchestration) and the `Push`/`PullSync` RPCs are deleted when the migration completes — owner, 2026-07-05: "ONE BLOCK OF CODE that does the transfer. no POSSIBILITY OF ANYTHING EVER using anything else because anything else does not exist"; "I NEVER see a situation where pull is faster than push or vice versa... because of something blit did." Benchmark methodology corollary: cross-direction performance comparisons are valid only on symmetric endpoints ("tmp on one side, spinning rust on the other is not a valid test"); tmpfs cells are wire-reference only. Plan: `docs/plan/ONE_TRANSFER_PATH.md` (Draft; no code until the owner flips it Active). `docs/plan/SMALL_FILE_CEILING.md` is **paused** effective immediately at sf-2 (done): sf-3a and later slices are blocked until ONE_TRANSFER_PATH ships, then resume/re-derive against the unified baseline (owner delegated this sequencing: "I DO NOT CARE. FIX IT.").
.review/results/one-transfer-path-plan.codex.md:1765:   132	- Supersedes: the post-REV4 residue item "pull 1s-start restructuring" (STATE Queue item 4 — absorbed by ONE_TRANSFER_PATH's streaming-manifest choreography); SMALL_FILE_CEILING's queue position (paused, not superseded — its principle D-2026-07-04-4 stands).

.review/results/one-transfer-path-plan.codex.md:2124:- **[High] [ONE_TRANSFER_PATH §Non-goals](/home/michael/dev/Blit/docs/plan/ONE_TRANSFER_PATH.md:46) vs [REV4 §Constraints](/home/michael/dev/Blit/docs/plan/UNIFIED_TRANSFER_ENGINE_REV4.md:86)**: The draft says pre-plan wire compatibility is a non-goal and `Push`/`PullSync` are deleted at lockstep cutover, but Active REV4 still requires mixed old/new peers to negotiate down. D-2026-07-05-1 does not explicitly supersede that REV4 compatibility rule, so two authorities conflict.
.review/results/one-transfer-path-plan.codex.md:2126:- **[Medium] [ONE_TRANSFER_PATH §Constraints/Design](/home/michael/dev/Blit/docs/plan/ONE_TRANSFER_PATH.md:73) vs [REV4 §Dial](/home/michael/dev/Blit/docs/plan/UNIFIED_TRANSFER_ENGINE_REV4.md:318)**: The plan makes sf-2 shape-correction “the one and only stream policy” but does not pin the bounded-unilateral dial contract: receiver capacity profile, rich profile fields, and sender-owned dial within receiver ceiling. That is required by D-2026-06-20-1/-2 and should be in otp-1/acceptance.
.review/results/one-transfer-path-plan.codex.md:2128:- **[Medium] [ONE_TRANSFER_PATH §Acceptance/Slices](/home/michael/dev/Blit/docs/plan/ONE_TRANSFER_PATH.md:95)**: The deletion proof omits the current `DelegatedPull` RPC even though [proto/blit.proto](/home/michael/dev/Blit/proto/blit.proto:43) describes it as a daemon-to-daemon byte path. If it remains only as an auth/control gate, say so and prove it carries no bytes; otherwise include it in the deletion proof. Also align the remote deletion slice with local orchestration deletion in otp-10.
.review/results/one-transfer-path-plan.codex.md:2130:- **[Medium] [ONE_TRANSFER_PATH §Constraints/Acceptance](/home/michael/dev/Blit/docs/plan/ONE_TRANSFER_PATH.md:65)**: “Converge up, not down” is a constraint but not an observable acceptance criterion. The final checklist only proves initiator/verb invariance; a symmetric-but-slower unified path could pass. Add a symmetric pre-cutover baseline and require final cells to match the better same-rig direction within noise.
.review/results/one-transfer-path-plan.codex.md:2132:- **[Medium] [ONE_TRANSFER_PATH §Design](/home/michael/dev/Blit/docs/plan/ONE_TRANSFER_PATH.md:137)**: Resume ordering is underspecified. The choreography opens the data plane immediately, then names resume as an optional block-hash phase, but does not state when destination block maps/partial-file validation must complete or whether resume is a RELIABLE exception to immediate send. otp-1/otp-6 should pin ordering and stale-partial failure cases.
.review/results/one-transfer-path-plan.codex.md:2134:VERDICT: NEEDS FIXES before Active flip. No quiet re-litigation found for the ceiling principle, codex loop, or zero-copy decision; the main issues are unresolved REV4 compatibility/dial carryover and missing proof points around deletion and acceptance.
.review/results/one-transfer-path-plan.codex.md:2137:- **[High] [ONE_TRANSFER_PATH §Non-goals](/home/michael/dev/Blit/docs/plan/ONE_TRANSFER_PATH.md:46) vs [REV4 §Constraints](/home/michael/dev/Blit/docs/plan/UNIFIED_TRANSFER_ENGINE_REV4.md:86)**: The draft says pre-plan wire compatibility is a non-goal and `Push`/`PullSync` are deleted at lockstep cutover, but Active REV4 still requires mixed old/new peers to negotiate down. D-2026-07-05-1 does not explicitly supersede that REV4 compatibility rule, so two authorities conflict.
.review/results/one-transfer-path-plan.codex.md:2139:- **[Medium] [ONE_TRANSFER_PATH §Constraints/Design](/home/michael/dev/Blit/docs/plan/ONE_TRANSFER_PATH.md:73) vs [REV4 §Dial](/home/michael/dev/Blit/docs/plan/UNIFIED_TRANSFER_ENGINE_REV4.md:318)**: The plan makes sf-2 shape-correction “the one and only stream policy” but does not pin the bounded-unilateral dial contract: receiver capacity profile, rich profile fields, and sender-owned dial within receiver ceiling. That is required by D-2026-06-20-1/-2 and should be in otp-1/acceptance.
.review/results/one-transfer-path-plan.codex.md:2141:- **[Medium] [ONE_TRANSFER_PATH §Acceptance/Slices](/home/michael/dev/Blit/docs/plan/ONE_TRANSFER_PATH.md:95)**: The deletion proof omits the current `DelegatedPull` RPC even though [proto/blit.proto](/home/michael/dev/Blit/proto/blit.proto:43) describes it as a daemon-to-daemon byte path. If it remains only as an auth/control gate, say so and prove it carries no bytes; otherwise include it in the deletion proof. Also align the remote deletion slice with local orchestration deletion in otp-10.
.review/results/one-transfer-path-plan.codex.md:2143:- **[Medium] [ONE_TRANSFER_PATH §Constraints/Acceptance](/home/michael/dev/Blit/docs/plan/ONE_TRANSFER_PATH.md:65)**: “Converge up, not down” is a constraint but not an observable acceptance criterion. The final checklist only proves initiator/verb invariance; a symmetric-but-slower unified path could pass. .review/results/one-transfer-path-plan.codex.md:2145:- **[Medium] [ONE_TRANSFER_PATH §Design](/home/michael/dev/Blit/docs/plan/ONE_TRANSFER_PATH.md:137)**: Resume ordering is underspecified. .review/results/one-transfer-path-plan.codex.md:2147:VERDICT: NEEDS FIXES before Active flip. No quiet re-litigation found for the ceiling principle, codex loop, or zero-copy decision; the main issues are unresolved REV4 compatibility/dial carryover and missing proof points around deletion and acceptance.
.review/results/sf-2-shape-correction-resize.codex.md:6:provider: openai
.review/results/sf-2-shape-correction-resize.codex.md:14:Review the diff of commit c70c2ac (run: git show c70c2ac). It implements sf-2 of docs/plan/SMALL_FILE_CEILING.md (Active): client-side shape-correction stream resize — the daemon proposes the push epoch-0 stream count from a partial manifest at its early flush, so many-tiny-file pushes rode 1 stream; the client now re-runs the engine shape table (initial_stream_proposal) over the accumulated need list and corrects upward via the existing ue-r2-2 resize wire, one ADD epoch at a time. Check: correctness regressions, the slice's acceptance criteria (proposal-table unit pins for the plan's cells + loopback e2e pin that a 10k-file push opens >1 data-plane connection), FAST/SIMPLE/RELIABLE, the invariants relevant to transfer code (byte-identical / StallGuard / cancellation / byte-accounting), interaction between the two resize proposers (tuner resize_tick vs propose_shape_resize — epoch allocation, one-in-flight, flap risk), mixed-version behavior (old daemon / gRPC fallback unchanged), and that the test count did not drop (1479 -> 1483). Output a concise markdown findings list — each finding with file:line, severity, rationale — then a final VERDICT line. Be concise; do not invoke skills.

.review/results/sf-2-shape-correction-resize.codex.md:4928:.review/results/d-55-f3-mirror.reopened.md:16:   `spawn_f3_pull` builds the TUI mirror execution with `options: PullSyncOptions::default()` at `crates/blit-tui/src/main.rs:2881` through `crates/blit-tui/src/main.rs:2886`. The separate `PullSyncExecution.mirror_mode` field is passed to `RemotePullClient::pull_sync` as `track_paths` at `crates/blit-app/src/transfers/remote.rs:343` through `crates/blit-app/src/transfers/remote.rs:349`, but the wire `TransferOperationSpec` is built only from `execution.options` at `crates/blit-core/src/remote/pull.rs:598` through `crates/blit-core/src/remote/pull.rs:606`. Since `PullSyncOptions::default().mirror_mode` is false, `build_spec_from_options` emits `MirrorMode::Off` at `crates/blit-core/src/remote/pull.rs:558` through `crates/blit-core/src/remote/pull.rs:565`.
